items.title: 鞍山人不爱说话，有事都写墙上
items.transcript: 厂子老大了个了
items.translation_en: The factory is absolutely massive.
items.description: The use of the '老...个了' structure is a classic intensifier in Northeastern Mandarin.

items.title: 鞍山人不爱说话，有事都写墙上
items.transcript: 不爱支声
items.translation_en: They don't like to speak up.
items.description: '支声' (zhī shēng) is a regional colloquialism used in the Northeast to mean 'to speak' or 'to make a sound'.

items.title: 鞍山人不爱说话，有事都写墙上
items.transcript: 蘸老香了
items.translation_en: It tastes incredibly good.
items.description: Using '老' (lǎo) as an adverb for 'very' is a hallmark of Dongbei speech.

items.title: 鞍山人不爱说话，有事都写墙上
items.transcript: 是一样字儿的
items.translation_en: It is exactly the same.
items.description: The replacement of the common suffix '子' (zi) with '字儿' (zìr) is a specific phonetic feature often associated with the Anshan dialect.

items.title: 鞍山人不爱说话，有事都写墙上
items.transcript: 咱不行也到边上脑五下子得了
items.translation_en: Otherwise we can just do a quick visit on the side.
items.description: The word '脑' (nǎo) is used here as a dialectal verb for performing an action briefly or roughly.

items.title: 不是你标准的东莞话就不能说了吗?
items.transcript: 朗礼，唔係讲清提，係讲“时”。你嘶唔知朗礼咩意思呀？
items.translation_en: Lang-li, we don't say Qing-ti, we say 'Shi'. Do you know what Lang-li means?
items.description: Uses specific Dongguan regional terms for time of day, contrasting 'Lang-li' with more common terms.

items.title: 不是你标准的东莞话就不能说了吗?
items.transcript: 咩叫朗礼呀？朗礼呀，朗礼呀，係街告晨晨呀。
items.translation_en: What is Lang-li? Lang-li, Lang-li, it's early morning on the street.
items.description: Features an elderly speaker using authentic local pronunciation and the specific term 'Lang-li' for morning.

items.title: 不是你标准的东莞话就不能说了吗?
items.transcript: 咁朗两呢？朗两呀，晏昼呀。
items.translation_en: How about Lang-liang? Lang-liang is the afternoon.
items.description: Uses 'Lang-liang', a distinct Dongguan dialectal term for the afternoon period.

items.title: 不是你标准的东莞话就不能说了吗?
items.transcript: 好眼，即係好賴澀。
items.translation_en: 'Hou-ngaan', which means very filthy.
items.description: Explains a unique Dongguan adjective '好眼' (hou-ngaan) used to describe something dirty or messy.

items.title: 不是你标准的东莞话就不能说了吗?
items.transcript: 好行塞，即係好污糟嘅意思。
items.translation_en: 'Hou-hang-sak', meaning the same as very dirty.
items.description: Presents another local dialect term '好行塞' (hou-hang-sak) for 'dirty' or 'filthy'.

items.title: 你以为只是搞笑的东北话，其实是一座语言博物馆！
items.transcript: 哎呀妈呀，咋整的呀？干哈呢？溜达溜达去？
items.translation_en: Oh my god, how did this happen? What are you doing? Going for a stroll?
items.description: Includes quintessential Dongbei interrogatives like '咋整' and '干哈' along with characteristic exclamations.

items.title: 你以为只是搞笑的东北话，其实是一座语言博物馆！
items.transcript: 您说秃噜反仗，别人立马就知道这人不靠谱。
items.translation_en: If you say 'tulu fanzhang', others will immediately know the person is unreliable.
items.description: The term '秃噜反仗' is a highly specific regional idiom for inconsistency or messiness.

items.title: 你以为只是搞笑的东北话，其实是一座语言博物馆！
items.transcript: 五脊六兽，那种浑身不得劲百无聊赖的感觉。
items.translation_en: Wuji liushou, that feeling of being restless and incredibly bored.
items.description: A classic Northeast Chinese expression describing a specific state of restlessness or ennui.

items.title: 你以为只是搞笑的东北话，其实是一座语言博物馆！
items.transcript: 上街溜达，咱们这儿会说溜达该去。
items.translation_en: To go for a stroll on the street, we say 'liu da gai qu' here.
items.description: Features the characteristic phonetic shift where 'jie' (street) is pronounced as 'gai', a trait of the dialect.

items.title: 你以为只是搞笑的东北话，其实是一座语言博物馆！
items.transcript: 形容数量特别的多，您说老鼻子了。
items.translation_en: To describe a very large quantity, you say 'lao bizi le'.
items.description: The phrase '老鼻子' is a unique and widely used Northeast slang term meaning 'a huge amount'.

items.title: 泠鸢讲四专 宜宾方言推荐看《宇宙探索编辑部》
items.transcript: 呢萨里...呢萨里...呢萨里加
items.translation_en: Nian sa li... Nian sa li... Nian sa li jia.
items.description: Uses specific tonal shifts and phonetic variations characteristic of the Yibin regional accent.

items.title: 泠鸢讲四专 宜宾方言推荐看《宇宙探索编辑部》
items.transcript: 路土给散...路土给散...哎呀呀,我这个宜宾话已经被成都话影响太重了
items.translation_en: Lu tu ge san... Lu tu ge san... Oh dear, my Yibin dialect has been too heavily influenced by the Chengdu accent.
items.description: The speaker is explicitly attempting Yibin pronunciation and comments on the linguistic interference from Chengdu dialect.

items.title: 泠鸢讲四专 宜宾方言推荐看《宇宙探索编辑部》
items.transcript: 喔,博灰话
items.translation_en: Oh, Bo-hui-hua.
items.description: Features a heavy regional accent and specific local phonetic styling.

items.title: 泠鸢讲四专 宜宾方言推荐看《宇宙探索编辑部》
items.transcript: 我有些时候有点嫌弃成都这边那个宜宾燃面的那个面,有点太绵了
items.translation_en: Sometimes I'm a bit critical of the Yibin 'ranmian' noodles here in Chengdu; the noodles are a bit too mushy.
items.description: Discussion of local culinary specialities using regional terminology like 'mian' (soft/mushy) with a Sichuan cadence.

items.title: 河北衡水市各地方言，你能听懂几个？
items.transcript: 要塞，我这不那阳人吗？我种了地，种了茄子，种了蒜苔，嘎地儿没活的时候勒，我就挑着棒子，搞点粮食。
items.translation_en: Wait, aren't I from that place? I farmed the land, planted eggplants, planted garlic sprouts... when there was no work in the fields, I carried poles to get some grain.
items.description: The Shenzhou City dialect features unique particles like 'yàosai' and specific regional pronunciations for people and farming activities.

items.title: 河北衡水市各地方言，你能听懂几个？
items.transcript: 大老爷说，上灯台，偷油吃，下不来，紫了紫了叫奶奶，奶奶不抱，吭哧一跳。
items.translation_en: The master says, go up the lamp stand, steal oil to eat, can't come down, crying for grandma, grandma doesn't hold me, jump down with a thud.
items.description: This Jinxian nursery rhyme is recited with a heavy local accent and specific regional vocabulary for the actions.

items.title: 河北衡水市各地方言，你能听懂几个？
items.transcript: 我是河北的，河北衡水的，衡水武强的，要想过得好，总往武强跑。
items.translation_en: I'm from Hebei, Hengshui, Hebei, Wuqiang, Hengshui. If you want a good life, keep running to Wuqiang.
items.description: A rhythmic chant in the Wuqiang County dialect, demonstrating the local intonation and phonetic style typical of the Hebei region.

items.title: 河北衡水市各地方言，你能听懂几个？
items.transcript: 咱们爱吃的太，河北衡水枣强的，四传的辣，让你吃辣。啥西的醋，让你点面。
items.translation_en: The food we love, it's from Zaoqiang, Hengshui, Hebei. Sichuan's spice makes you eat spicy. Shanxi's vinegar makes you dip noodles.
items.description: Zaoqiang County dialect with strong local emphasis on specific words like 'tai' for food and distinctive Hebei-style tonal shifts.

items.title: 河北衡水市各地方言，你能听懂几个？
items.transcript: 咱衡水武邑那么多年，你说明显吧，那家一个事儿，整天忙忙火火的。
items.translation_en: In our Wuyi, Hengshui for so many years, you say it's obvious, right? One family thing after another, busy all day.
items.description: Wuyi County dialect features colloquialisms like 'máng máng huǒ huǒ' and specific phonetic endings characteristic of the area.

items.title: 【山东威海话】无入声方言朗读《郭沫若没出息》(胶辽官话-登连片-烟威小片-环翠)（1.15倍速）
items.transcript: 郭沫若没出息。不记得多少岁，夜里和郭世黎早，读书读不到步。
items.translation_en: Guo Moruo had no prospects. Not remembering how old he was, at night he and Guo Shili were up early, but reading books got him nowhere.
items.description: Features the typical Weihai 'third tone' which sounds lower/different from Standard Mandarin, and the local pronunciation of '不' and '读'.

items.title: 【山东威海话】无入声方言朗读《郭沫若没出息》(胶辽官话-登连片-烟威小片-环翠)（1.15倍速）
items.transcript: 这一股小自傲，过程出不和，极地不利。发怒是指，嘻嘻嘘嘘。
items.translation_en: This little bit of self-pride, things weren't harmonious, extremely unfavorable. Pointing in anger, whispering and shushing.
items.description: The pronunciation of '过程' and '极地' demonstrates the unique vowel qualities and tonal shifts of the Yan-Wei small patch dialect.

items.title: 【山东威海话】无入声方言朗读《郭沫若没出息》(胶辽官话-登连片-烟威小片-环翠)（1.15倍速）
items.transcript: 这卧床的老婆媳妇，可不是流出石油，给女上马雇。哈耶克美，绝不哈白色里涩皮。
items.translation_en: This bedridden wife, it's not like oil flowing out, hiring horses for women. Hayek is beautiful, never like that white leather.
items.description: Strong dialectal phrasing and the phonetic shift in words like '媳妇' and '白色' are indicative of the Jiao-Liao Mandarin group.

items.title: 【山东威海话】无入声方言朗读《郭沫若没出息》(胶辽官话-登连片-烟威小片-环翠)（1.15倍速）
items.transcript: 不服法法，一月月，几周年，两和陆亚好却。极其，我在给啥路，才起些。
items.translation_en: Not obeying the law, month after month, for several years... Extremely, what road am I giving, only then rising a bit.
items.description: The use of '啥' combined with the distinct falling-rising tone sandhi of Weihai speech is evident here.

items.title: 【山东威海话】无入声方言朗读《郭沫若没出息》(胶辽官话-登连片-烟威小片-环翠)（1.15倍速）
items.transcript: 这几亩岁不之余，那是雅乐，抹珠、抹目、也抹媳，特别卡。一于最漫的苦。
items.translation_en: In these few years, that was elegant music, wiping pearls, eyes, and the wife, very difficult. A surplus of the slowest bitterness.
items.description: Includes regional vocabulary like '不之余' and the local pronunciation of '抹' (wiping), which differs significantly from Standard Mandarin.

items.title: 示范课1《方言翻译器》授课教师：李超（长春净月高新技术产业开发区华岳学校）第二部分
items.transcript: 膝盖
items.translation_en: Knee
items.description: The student pronounces the word with the distinct tonal qualities and 'er-hua' (rhoticity) common in the Changchun dialect of Northeastern Mandarin.

items.title: 示范课1《方言翻译器》授课教师：李超（长春净月高新技术产业开发区华岳学校）第二部分
items.transcript: 咋回事
items.translation_en: What's going on?
items.description: The use of '咋' (zǎ) instead of '怎么' (zěnme) is a core lexical feature of Northeastern Mandarin.

items.title: 示范课1《方言翻译器》授课教师：李超（长春净月高新技术产业开发区华岳学校）第二部分
items.transcript: 膝盖
items.translation_en: Knee
items.description: During the model testing phase, the student uses a strong regional accent to verify the software's recognition of the dialect.

items.title: 示范课1《方言翻译器》授课教师：李超（长春净月高新技术产业开发区华岳学校）第二部分
items.transcript: 咋回事
items.translation_en: What's the matter?
items.description: The student tests the 'Dongbei' category in the application using the local colloquialism '咋回事'.

items.title: 潍坊佩奇：我喜欢划船！
items.transcript: 大家好，俺是来自山东潍坊的五花。
items.translation_en: Hello everyone, I am Wuhua from Weifang, Shandong.
items.description: The use of '俺' (an) as the first-person singular pronoun is a hallmark of the Shandong/Weifang dialect.

items.title: 潍坊佩奇：我喜欢划船！
items.transcript: 恁来自潍坊的，不会游泳怕水的，欢迎来到船上哈。
items.translation_en: You folks from Weifang who can't swim and are afraid of water, welcome aboard.
items.description: The use of '恁' (nin) for 'you' and the specific intonation are characteristic of the regional speech in Weifang.

items.title: 潍坊佩奇：我喜欢划船！
items.transcript: 没有事，俺死猪又不怕开水烫。
items.translation_en: It's fine, I'm a dead pig and not afraid of boiling water.
items.description: Uses the dialect pronoun '俺' combined with local colloquial phrasing and accent patterns.

items.title: 潍坊佩奇：我喜欢划船！
items.transcript: 俺娘欸，这么小的堆儿，我上岛上栽点什么煮食都费劲。
items.translation_en: My goodness, such a small mound, it's hard to even plant some food on this island.
items.description: '俺娘欸' (An niang ei) is a very common regional exclamation in Shandong used to express surprise or frustration.

items.title: 潍坊佩奇：我喜欢划船！
items.transcript: 俺娘欸，登船真是个力气活，真真累死我了。
items.translation_en: My goodness, rowing is really hard work, I'm truly exhausted.
items.description: Features the signature '俺娘欸' exclamation and the specific heavy rhoticity (Er-hua) found in the local accent.

items.title: 给大家整点花活，株洲白关话，奇志大宾双簧
items.transcript: 哈里欧开了一个洗脚城，地点就在金盆岭，金盆洗脚。
items.translation_en: Harry Ou opened a foot massage parlor, the location is right at Jinpenling, 'Golden Basin Foot Washing'.
items.description: Features local Zhuzhou phonology and specific regional place names like Jinpenling.

items.title: 给大家整点花活，株洲白关话，奇志大宾双簧
items.transcript: 洗脚城赚贱呢，我洗一个脚，最少它五十块，我洗一百个脚，赚好几千呐。
items.translation_en: Foot massage parlors make easy money; I wash one pair of feet, that's at least fifty yuan, I wash a hundred pairs, and I've made several thousand.
items.description: The use of '赚贱' (easy/cheap money) and the specific cadence is characteristic of regional Hunan dialects.

items.title: 给大家整点花活，株洲白关话，奇志大宾双簧
items.transcript: 你给我出去！你个老板什么法财了，怎么的啦？
items.translation_en: Get out of here! You boss, what kind of fortune are you making? What's going on?
items.description: Uses local interrogative structures and the distinct accent of the Zhuzhou region.

items.title: 给大家整点花活，株洲白关话，奇志大宾双簧
items.transcript: 我跟具体个脚啊，哈里的，我喷具体送脚一开呐，那真是，飞了老头子跳高啊，哈老子一跳。
items.translation_en: I was washing this specific foot, Harry's, and as soon as I opened it up, it was like an old man doing a high jump, it gave me a real fright.
items.description: Contains vivid local idioms like '飞了老头子跳高' and dialectal particles.

items.title: 给大家整点花活，株洲白关话，奇志大宾双簧
items.transcript: 某得，你问他去么子切么子，切么子不知道，反正第二天隔壁就关门的。
items.translation_en: Nothing, you ask him what he's going for or what he's eating, no one knows what he's eating, but anyway the next day the place next door closed.
items.description: Includes '么子' (what), a signature interrogative pronoun in Hunan dialects, and specific local verb pronunciations.

items.title: 【闽南语】台湾闽南语新竹市区腔与北港腔
items.transcript: 我个囡仔喔，唔免講彼個時陣喔，愛讀到十二點外咧，你知唔？我攏猶未睏，我嘛猶未睏捏。
items.translation_en: My child, back then, he had to study until past twelve o'clock, you know? I hadn't slept yet, and neither had he.
items.description: Uses the Hsinchu urban accent of Taiwanese Hokkien, characterized by specific vowel shifts and intonation patterns distinct from standard Taiwanese.

items.title: 【闽南语】台湾闽南语新竹市区腔与北港腔
items.transcript: 我个囡仔喔，真巧。學校到處，處到學校，伊唔曾提零用錢，唔曾共我討錢捏。
items.translation_en: My child is very smart. From school to home, and home to school, he never took pocket money or asked me for money.
items.description: A clear example of Hsinchu urban Minnan dialect used in a personal narrative context.

items.title: 【闽南语】台湾闽南语新竹市区腔与北港腔
items.transcript: 咱北港的大餅，由來啊，就是咱自古農業時代，咱北港有名的名產就是麻油，唔是就係蠶豆、土豆按呢。
items.translation_en: The origin of our Beigang big cakes is from the old agricultural era. Our famous local products in Beigang are sesame oil, broad beans, and peanuts.
items.description: Displays the Beigang accent, which has a distinct phonetic profile (often associated with 'coastal' or Quanzhou-leaning traits) compared to the Hsinchu accent.

items.title: 【闽南语】台湾闽南语新竹市区腔与北港腔
items.transcript: 來到北港啊，就是佇咱朝天宮咱媽祖婆這周圍，大概有二、三十間咧賣餅。
items.translation_en: When you come to Beigang, around our Chaotian Temple and Mazu, there are about twenty or thirty shops selling these cakes.
items.description: Beigang regional speech used to describe local geography and commerce, showing regional variations in Hokkien.

items.title: 介绍上海话中一种独特的词缀
items.transcript: 今朝拨大家讲一讲上海闲话里向一种交关特别个后缀“sæ”。
items.translation_en: Today I will introduce to you a very special suffix in Shanghainese: 'se'.
items.description: The speaker uses Shanghainese pronunciation and grammar ('闲话里向', '拨大家讲') to introduce the topic.

items.title: 介绍上海话中一种独特的词缀
items.transcript: 比如讲：来sæ、潽sæ、昂sæ、落sæ、怪sæ、辣sæ、腰sæ...
items.translation_en: For example: lese, pese, anse, laose, guaise, lase, yaose...
items.description: Lists specific Shanghainese words ending in the 'se' suffix that are characteristic of the dialect.

items.title: 介绍上海话中一种独特的词缀
items.transcript: 潽sæ，个个“潽”字勒上海闲话里向是个“潽、潽、潽、潽”，相当于浪费了。
items.translation_en: 'Pese', this word 'pe' in Shanghainese means 'overflow' or 'spill', implying something is wasted or lost.
items.description: Explains a specific dialectal term using Shanghainese phonetic examples for the character 'pe'.

items.title: 介绍上海话中一种独特的词缀
items.transcript: 比如讲：潽sæ，像sæ“backside”个音，昂sæ像“on sale”个音。
items.translation_en: For example, 'pese' sounds like 'backside', and 'anse' sounds like 'on sale'.
items.description: Discusses the popular theory of English transliterations influencing Shanghainese vocabulary.

items.title: 介绍上海话中一种独特的词缀
items.transcript: 上海人讲“茄sæ话”，就是对应上海本地闲话里向个“茄话”。
items.translation_en: Shanghainese people say 'ga se wu', which corresponds to 'ga wu' in local dialect.
items.description: Uses a very common Shanghainese colloquialism ('茄话' or '茄sæ话') to illustrate the linguistic point.

items.title: 拆！遵义专属「城市盲盒」：4.20上海路凤凰大酒店凭方言暗号领“心动线索卡”
items.transcript: 你忙得不赢，快跟我介绍哈子嘛。
items.translation_en: You're so busy, hurry up and tell me about it.
items.description: The usage of 'ha zi' as a suffix for 'a bit' and 'bu ying' to indicate high intensity are typical features of Zunyi/Southwestern Mandarin.

items.title: 拆！遵义专属「城市盲盒」：4.20上海路凤凰大酒店凭方言暗号领“心动线索卡”
items.transcript: 企事业单位嘞，真得吗？扎了。
items.translation_en: From public institutions, really? That's impressive!
items.description: The phrase 'za liao' is a highly local Zunyi slang term used to express admiration or that something is cool/impressive.

items.title: 拆！遵义专属「城市盲盒」：4.20上海路凤凰大酒店凭方言暗号领“心动线索卡”
items.transcript: 哇领，好啊，啷个参加呀？
items.translation_en: Wow, that's great! How do I join?
items.description: Uses 'nang ge' (啷个) instead of the standard Mandarin 'zen me' (怎么) for 'how'.

items.title: 榆林搞笑段子
items.transcript: 这瓜打得什么风把你给吹来了
items.translation_en: What kind of wind blew you here today?
items.description: Uses regional phrasing and pronunciation typical of Northern Shaanxi/Jin dialects.

items.title: 榆林搞笑段子
items.transcript: 我就是来看看你们过得好着吧
items.translation_en: I just came to see if you guys are doing well.
items.description: The usage of '好着吧' (hao zhe ba) reflects local grammatical particles and intonation.

items.title: 榆林搞笑段子
items.transcript: 你说你找个比我年轻的我还被也乎
items.translation_en: If you found someone younger than me, I'd be confused/jealous.
items.description: Contains the dialectal term '也乎' (ye hu), which is specific to the region's vocabulary.

items.title: 榆林搞笑段子
items.transcript: 你少给你外面的好猫猫说上点
items.translation_en: Stop talking to those little 'kittens' (mistresses) you have outside.
items.description: The term '好猫猫' (hao mao mao) is a local slang term used in the Yulin/Ordos area.

items.title: 榆林搞笑段子
items.transcript: 十里花铁街对面金河毛发健康管理
items.translation_en: Across from Shilihua Iron Street, Jinhe Hair Health Management.
items.description: The rapid-fire delivery and vowel shifts are characteristic of the localized Jin dialect in Northern Shaanxi.

items.title: 傻儿师长重庆方言版
items.transcript: 傻儿傻儿，三十万大洋不要，你疯了！哦，你可是想把钱全部收下呀。
items.translation_en: Sha'er, Sha'er, you don't want 300,000 silver dollars? You're crazy! Oh, so you want to take all the money yourself.
items.description: Uses distinctive Chongqing tones and the character address 'Sha'er' which is a local moniker.

items.title: 傻儿师长重庆方言版
items.transcript: 送上门的财喜都不要，你才是个真正的好儿。
items.translation_en: You don't even want the fortune delivered to your door; you are a truly good man.
items.description: Features the 'er-hua' suffix typical of the dialect and specific regional phrasing for 'good person'.

items.title: 傻儿师长重庆方言版
items.transcript: 副团长是个啥子东西？晓得！
items.translation_en: What kind of thing is a deputy commander? We know!
items.description: Includes '啥子' (shazi) for 'what' and '晓得' (xiaode) for 'know', which are classic Southwestern Mandarin/Chongqing markers.

items.title: 傻儿师长重庆方言版
items.transcript: 哈哥，你好眼力，反正你要学我这，这戏就是见识。
items.translation_en: Brother Ha, you have a good eye. Anyway, if you want to learn from me, this act is all about experience.
items.description: The term '哈哥' (Ha Ge) and the heavy nasalization and tonal shifts are characteristic of the Chongqing accent.

items.title: 傻儿师长重庆方言版
items.transcript: 我们屋头有的是粮食。谁记是当皇姑也没得啥子的。
items.translation_en: We have plenty of grain at home. It doesn't matter who the imperial aunt is.
items.description: Uses '屋头' (wutou) meaning 'home' and '没得啥子' (meide shazi) meaning 'it's nothing/no problem'.

items.title: 【桂林彩调】越看越有意思的三看亲
items.transcript: 哦！王媒婆，你不得了哦。
items.translation_en: Oh! Matchmaker Wang, you are quite something.
items.description: Uses the characteristic Guilin dialect intonation and the sentence-final particle '哦' common in local speech.

items.title: 【桂林彩调】越看越有意思的三看亲
items.transcript: 我讲讲讲，那是金妹妹呀，哎哟，俏得很呢。
items.translation_en: I'm saying, that's Sister Jin, oh, she is very pretty.
items.description: The phrase '俏得很' (very pretty/smart) and the rhythmic repetition are typical of the Guilin Caidiao style.

items.title: 【桂林彩调】越看越有意思的三看亲
items.transcript: 王官，今天我约好多人想过一气看清。
items.translation_en: Wang Guan, today I invited many people to go take a look together.
items.description: Includes local vocabulary like '一气' (together) and Guilin-specific phonetic patterns.

items.title: 【桂林彩调】越看越有意思的三看亲
items.transcript: 三看亲啰，一排相边，不是腰里长。
items.translation_en: Three meetings of the in-laws, lined up side by side, it's not a long waist.
items.description: The usage of '啰' and the specific phrasing for the 'three meetings' ritual reflect Guilin regional culture.

items.title: 【蜡嘴雀】越受伤，越坚强~
items.transcript: 哎，你个叼人，你
items.translation_en: Hey, you rascal, you.
items.description: The term '叼人' (diāorén) is a distinctive slang expression used in Hunan dialects, including Yueyang, to describe someone who is being mischievous or difficult in a playful or mock-scolding manner.

items.title: 【蜡嘴雀】越受伤，越坚强~
items.transcript: 起不来了，哈
items.translation_en: Can't get up, huh?
items.description: The specific use of the final particle '哈' combined with the local intonation is typical of the Yueyang region's informal spoken language.

items.title: 周深深圳港广东话猴赛雷！好苏吼温柔吼吼听叻！
items.transcript: 大家好啊
items.translation_en: Hello everyone
items.description: Standard Cantonese greeting used to open the concert interaction.

items.title: 周深深圳港广东话猴赛雷！好苏吼温柔吼吼听叻！
items.transcript: 我係周深
items.translation_en: I am Zhou Shen
items.description: Self-introduction using the Cantonese verb 'hai' (to be) and Cantonese pronunciation of his name.

items.title: 周深深圳港广东话猴赛雷！好苏吼温柔吼吼听叻！
items.transcript: 講廣東話咯，唔係好識講
items.translation_en: Speaking Cantonese, I don't know how to speak it very well
items.description: Uses the Cantonese negative marker 'm' and the verb 'sik' (to know how to), reflecting typical Cantonese grammar.

items.title: 周深深圳港广东话猴赛雷！好苏吼温柔吼吼听叻！
items.transcript: 咁...你哋識唔識講廣東話咯
items.translation_en: So... do you guys know how to speak Cantonese?
items.description: The sentence structure 'sik-m-sik' (know or not know) is a classic Cantonese interrogative form.

items.title: 周深深圳港广东话猴赛雷！好苏吼温柔吼吼听叻！
items.transcript: 講咩都好開心㗎
items.translation_en: Whatever I say, I'm very happy
items.description: Features the Cantonese interrogative/indefinite pronoun 'me' (what) and the sentence-final particle 'gaa'.

items.title: 看看那些中古全浊字现在多数读送气的粤语有哪些？「各地粤语並母字读法（7）合浦,北海,廉江,灵山,吴川,香港,儋州,三亚,东方」
items.transcript: 蓬, 皮, 盘
items.translation_en: Fluffy, skin, plate
items.description: The Sanya dialect exhibits specific vowel shifts and maintains certain aspiration patterns for characters originally belonging to the 'bing' initial group.

items.title: 看看那些中古全浊字现在多数读送气的粤语有哪些？「各地粤语並母字读法（7）合浦,北海,廉江,灵山,吴川,香港,儋州,三亚,东方」
items.transcript: 步, 倍, 盆
items.translation_en: Step, double, basin
items.description: These characters demonstrate the unique phonetic realization of voiced initials in the Sanya region compared to standard Cantonese.

items.title: 霸道总裁说方言要减多少分？
items.transcript: 你个四姐，四姐有个管哩
items.translation_en: Your fourth sister, what does she care?
items.description: The phrase uses the characteristically Wuhu modal particle '管哩' (guǎn li) and features the specific tonal and phonetic qualities of the Jiang-Huai Mandarin dialect.

items.title: 澳门动漫玩具街！澳门V带你逛10家动漫谷子店！下集【澳门VLOG】
items.transcript: 早晨呀老闆，大家好，我係彩梨呀。
items.translation_en: Good morning, everyone! I'm Choi Lei.
items.description: The speaker uses the Cantonese greeting '早晨' (jou san) and the address '老闆' (lou sai), characteristic of the Macau/Hong Kong dialect.

items.title: 澳门动漫玩具街！澳门V带你逛10家动漫谷子店！下集【澳门VLOG】
items.transcript: 噉我哋一出咗一鴻門口呢，隔籬就又有間扭蛋舖喇。
items.translation_en: Once we step out of the entrance of Yat Hung, there is another gachapon shop next door.
items.description: Uses Cantonese particles like '噉' (gam) and '隔籬' (gaak lei) for 'next door'.

items.title: 澳门动漫玩具街！澳门V带你逛10家动漫谷子店！下集【澳门VLOG】
items.transcript: 噉跟住下面呢度呢，就係我哋嘅抽業源喇。
items.translation_en: Next, right down here is our 'Chou Ye Yuan'.
items.description: Features Cantonese grammar and sentence-final particles typical of the region's speech.

items.title: 澳门动漫玩具街！澳门V带你逛10家动漫谷子店！下集【澳门VLOG】
items.transcript: 跟住我哋出咗抽業源往我哋嘅左邊，就見到一間開咗好耐嘅玩具舖叫行利玩具。
items.translation_en: Then, after leaving Chou Ye Yuan and heading to our left, we see a long-established toy shop called Hang Lei Toys.
items.description: Uses '開咗好耐' (hoi zo hou noi) to mean 'opened for a long time', which is specific to Cantonese syntax.

items.title: 澳门动漫玩具街！澳门V带你逛10家动漫谷子店！下集【澳门VLOG】
items.transcript: OK，噉今集嘅澳門動漫街Vlog下集就到呢度喇。
items.translation_en: OK, so that's the end of this second part of the Macau Anime Street VLOG.
items.description: Typical Cantonese closing phrasing using '到呢度喇' (dou ni dou laa).

items.title: 姐姐们的方言大挑战！听力考试现场，姐姐们说长治话你能听懂几句？
items.transcript: 肚子上长了一圈疙瘩咧
items.translation_en: A circle of bumps grew on the stomach.
items.description: The pronunciation of '疙瘩' (gēda) followed by the modal particle '咧' (liě) is characteristic of the local Jin dialect accent.

items.title: 姐姐们的方言大挑战！听力考试现场，姐姐们说长治话你能听懂几句？
items.transcript: 老汉干了点活，人那昂谢的
items.translation_en: The old man did some work and became very dirty.
items.description: The term '昂谢' (áng xiè) is a specific Changzhi dialect word meaning dirty, messy, or untidy.

items.title: 姐姐们的方言大挑战！听力考试现场，姐姐们说长治话你能听懂几句？
items.transcript: 我可原赖这儿搁溜得里耶
items.translation_en: I really like strolling around here.
items.description: The word '搁溜' (gē liū) is a dialectal verb for strolling or wandering around, and '原赖' (yuán lài) here is used as 'to like'.

items.title: 姐姐们的方言大挑战！听力考试现场，姐姐们说长治话你能听懂几句？
items.transcript: 我平时上脸颜色，都是比这种哈擦污烂的
items.translation_en: My usual facial complexion is even messier than this.
items.description: '哈擦污烂' (hā cā wū làn) is a highly specific regional colloquialism used to describe something very messy or in poor condition.

items.title: 大连话和东北话区别一下就听出来了吧
items.transcript: 在大连，俺们不说是你干哈呀，俺们说是你干哈么
items.translation_en: In Dalian, we don't say 'what are you doing' (Dongbei style), we say 'what are you doing' (Dalian style).
items.description: It contrasts the ending particle 'ya' used in general Dongbei Mandarin with the specific 'me' or 'ma' used in Dalian.

items.title: 大连话和东北话区别一下就听出来了吧
items.transcript: 俺们不说是七个八个，俺们说是起个叭个
items.translation_en: We don't say 'seven eight' (standard), we say 'seven eight' (Dalian tones).
items.description: Highlights the unique rising and falling tones in Dalian speech compared to standard Mandarin or Dongbei dialects.

items.title: 大连话和东北话区别一下就听出来了吧
items.transcript: 俺们也不说什么九个球，俺们说则个球
items.translation_en: We also don't say 'nine balls', we say 'nine balls' (ze-ge-qiu).
items.description: The pronunciation of the number nine ('jiu') as 'ze' is a classic characteristic of the Jiaoliao Mandarin spoken in Dalian.

items.title: 大连话和东北话区别一下就听出来了吧
items.transcript: 我真是渴了, 滋味啊
items.translation_en: I'm really thirsty, what's up/what's the matter.
items.description: Uses local slang ('ke-le' meaning 'speechless' or 'exhausted' in some contexts, and 'zi-mei-a' for 'what's the matter').

items.title: 哪句话在东北表示闲得无聊？
items.transcript: 什么屋脊六兽，也叫无脊六兽。
items.translation_en: Something like 'Wujiliushou', also called 'Wujiliushou' (idling on the roof ridge).
items.description: This is a classic Northeastern (Dongbei) idiom specifically used to describe extreme boredom or restlessness.

items.title: 哪句话在东北表示闲得无聊？
items.transcript: 后来龙王一看这不行啊，这天天闲得没事，游手好闲，这这不行。
items.translation_en: Later the Dragon King saw this wouldn't work; being idle every day with nothing to do, loafing around, this just won't do.
items.description: The phrase '闲得没事' combined with the repetitive storytelling rhythm is characteristic of the Dongbei dialectal style.

items.title: 哪句话在东北表示闲得无聊？
items.transcript: 你们六个就上屋脊上待着去吧，镇宅去。
items.translation_en: The six of you just go stay on the roof ridge, go protect the house.
items.description: The use of '待着去' (go stay/hang out) is a very common colloquialism in Northern and Northeastern dialects.

items.title: 锦州姑娘“质疑全场”，大秀锦州方言太搞笑
items.transcript: 你好吗？你是人吗？
items.translation_en: How are you? Are you a person?
items.description: Demonstrates the signature rising inflection of Jinzhou dialect that turns standard greetings into skeptical questions.

items.title: 锦州姑娘“质疑全场”，大秀锦州方言太搞笑
items.transcript: 我们？全世界都看我们？
items.translation_en: Us? The whole world is watching us?
items.description: An example of the 'questioning the world' trope associated with the Jinzhou accent, using heavy upward emphasis on the final syllables.

items.title: 锦州姑娘“质疑全场”，大秀锦州方言太搞笑
items.transcript: 香啊！招啊！
items.translation_en: Fragrant! Delicious!
items.description: Uses local emotive adjectives and the characteristic Jinzhou tone to describe the city's famous barbecue and seafood.

items.title: 锦州姑娘“质疑全场”，大秀锦州方言太搞笑
items.transcript: 喵？
items.translation_en: Meow?
items.description: A humorous imitation of a cat's meow using the Jinzhou rising intonation, symbolizing how the dialect permeates every sound.

items.title: 欢迎来嘉兴 我们在这里等你
items.transcript: 十一二点刚上挫，压力个斜拉噶嗦，昨日皮疹只多莫建挫，雷噶新的规矩十页规矩，到个我车中井位准...
items.translation_en: It's 11 or 12 o'clock and I just finished work, the pressure is so high. I saw so much yesterday, new rules and old rules, arriving at the city center...
items.description: Features distinctive Wu Chinese phonology, specifically the glottal stops and vowel shifts characteristic of the Jiaxing urban dialect.

items.title: 欢迎来嘉兴 我们在这里等你
items.transcript: 我讲嘉兴话真准，到不多不，嘉善、海宁、桐乡、海盐、平湖...
items.translation_en: I speak Jiaxing dialect very accurately, moving around Jiashan, Haining, Tongxiang, Haiyan, Pinghu...
items.description: The speaker explicitly identifies the language as 'Jiaxing dialect' and lists local county-level cities using local pronunciation.

items.title: 欢迎来嘉兴 我们在这里等你
items.transcript: 勒个心中个车中子，费农听港，不子嘉兴要老豆，三分钟一个城市...
items.translation_en: In the heart of the city center, don't listen to others, Jiaxing is getting older/better, one city every three minutes...
items.description: Uses local Wu grammar markers like '勒个' (at/in) and '港' (say/speak) instead of Mandarin equivalents.

items.title: 欢迎来嘉兴 我们在这里等你
items.transcript: 慢拉阔内瓦组，慢胜发可耐港夫，勒学后到阿拉桑府...
items.translation_en: Slowly look at this group, slowly develop the port, let's go to our prefecture...
items.description: The verse continues in the local Jiaxing dialect with high-speed rap delivery, maintaining consistent Wu tones.

items.title: 【自录自留】到底是谁教他的方言啊！！薛之谦呼和浩特演唱会唠嗑篇～
items.transcript: 也要眼吧
items.translation_en: Also need to perform, right?
items.description: The speaker uses a distinct regional tone and the final particle 'ba' in a way that mimics the Hohhot Jin dialect.

items.title: 【自录自留】到底是谁教他的方言啊！！薛之谦呼和浩特演唱会唠嗑篇～
items.transcript: 你们都莫我甩整的
items.translation_en: Don't you all mess with me.
items.description: The phrase 'shuai zheng' and the use of 'mo' are typical of the local colloquialisms used in the Hohhot region.

items.title: 【自录自留】到底是谁教他的方言啊！！薛之谦呼和浩特演唱会唠嗑篇～
items.transcript: 我看好多老袅袅躺在地上
items.translation_en: I see many old ladies lying on the ground.
items.description: The term 'lao niao niao' is a highly specific Hohhot dialect word referring to elderly women.

items.title: 【自录自留】到底是谁教他的方言啊！！薛之谦呼和浩特演唱会唠嗑篇～
items.transcript: 赶不上地铁了哇
items.translation_en: Can't catch the subway anymore!
items.description: The use of 'le wa' at the end of the sentence is the most characteristic feature of the Hohhot dialect (Jin Chinese influence).

items.title: 【自录自留】到底是谁教他的方言啊！！薛之谦呼和浩特演唱会唠嗑篇～
items.transcript: 骑不上马了哇
items.translation_en: Can't ride a horse anymore!
items.description: Combines the regional 'le wa' particle with local cultural stereotypes for a dialect-specific joke.

items.title: 兰州老乡集合这些方言老话你能听懂几个 #兰州方言 #兰州同城 #兰州 #快手本地生活 #百姓身边事
items.transcript: 白天有四风，晚上不哭大。
items.translation_en: During the day there are winds from all directions, but at night it doesn't howl.
items.description: The term 'kuda' is a specific Lanzhou dialect expression referring to the sound or action of the wind.

items.title: 兰州老乡集合这些方言老话你能听懂几个 #兰州方言 #兰州同城 #兰州 #快手本地生活 #百姓身边事
items.transcript: 吃饭不软人，最想炕等梦。
items.translation_en: Eating doesn't relax a person; what's desired most is dreaming on the heated brick bed.
items.description: Includes 'ruanren' and 'kang', reflecting local life and phonetic patterns of the Lanzhou region.

items.title: 兰州老乡集合这些方言老话你能听懂几个 #兰州方言 #兰州同城 #兰州 #快手本地生活 #百姓身边事
items.transcript: 炎天的破娘，草垫翻瓜的心。
items.translation_en: A woman in the heat of summer, a heart like a melon turned on a straw mat.
items.description: Uses 'poniang' and local metaphorical imagery common in Gansu folk sayings.

items.title: 兰州老乡集合这些方言老话你能听懂几个 #兰州方言 #兰州同城 #兰州 #快手本地生活 #百姓身边事
items.transcript: 做个啥事，都不往前边赶，就像一个死家嘎油子。
items.translation_en: No matter what you do, you never rush forward, just like a 'sijiagayouzi'.
items.description: The phrase 'sijiagayouzi' is a highly specific Lanzhou slang term for someone who is lazy or lacks initiative.

items.title: 太原马年雕塑连夜拆除！方言头逆马被恶搞，甘肃马踏飞燕赢麻了
items.transcript: 一位太原本地网友就在社交平台发言，表示这个造型像太原方言里的头逆马
items.translation_en: A local Taiyuan netizen posted on social media, stating this design resembles the 'tou ni ma' in the Taiyuan dialect.
items.description: The segment explicitly identifies a term from the Taiyuan dialect that locals used to mock the sculpture.

items.title: 太原马年雕塑连夜拆除！方言头逆马被恶搞，甘肃马踏飞燕赢麻了
items.transcript: 结果评论区又有人表示，这是卧头逆马
items.translation_en: As a result, someone in the comments section again remarked that this is 'wo tou ni ma'.
items.description: This phrase is a pun on the previously mentioned Taiyuan dialect term, adapted for when the sculpture was repositioned to be lying down.

items.title: 粤语名存实亡，乐坛港剧已死，以为撑得起？是广州的失守？是香港的沦陷？是粤港的内耗？谁来破局？
items.transcript: 做人如果冇夢想，同條鹹魚有咩分別？
items.translation_en: If a person doesn't have dreams, what's the difference between them and a salted fish?
items.description: Uses '冇' for 'not having' and '咩' for 'what', quintessential Cantonese colloquial grammar popularized by Stephen Chow films.

items.title: 粤语名存实亡，乐坛港剧已死，以为撑得起？是广州的失守？是香港的沦陷？是粤港的内耗？谁来破局？
items.transcript: 我要杯凍檸茶，走冰，多D糖。
items.translation_en: I want a glass of iced lemon tea, no ice, extra sugar.
items.description: Uses specific Hong Kong-style teahouse (cha chaan teng) terminology like '凍檸茶' and '走冰' (removing ice).

items.title: 粤语名存实亡，乐坛港剧已死，以为撑得起？是广州的失守？是香港的沦陷？是粤港的内耗？谁来破局？
items.transcript: 你要去粵劇院？左轉過三排樓，聽到梆黃聲就係咯。
items.translation_en: You want to go to the Cantonese Opera House? Turn left past three rows of buildings; where you hear the 'Banghuang' sound is it.
items.description: Contains '就係咯', a common Cantonese sentence-final particle cluster, and technical opera terms specific to the region.

items.title: 粤语名存实亡，乐坛港剧已死，以为撑得起？是广州的失守？是香港的沦陷？是粤港的内耗？谁来破局？
items.transcript: 最暖心的白話短語：等埋你，有我喺度，慢慢嚟。
items.translation_en: The most heartwarming Cantonese phrases: Wait for you, I'm here, take it slow.
items.description: Includes '等埋' (wait for) and '喺度' (to be here), which are structural markers unique to the Yue dialect.

items.title: 粤语名存实亡，乐坛港剧已死，以为撑得起？是广州的失守？是香港的沦陷？是粤港的内耗？谁来破局？
items.transcript: 粥煮好了，趁熱飲。
items.translation_en: The porridge is ready, drink it while it's hot.
items.description: Uses '飲' (yam) for consuming liquids/soup where Mandarin would use '喝' (he), reflecting ancient Chinese roots preserved in the dialect.

items.title: 苏州大小姐の拟声词挑战~💗
items.transcript: 咩咩咩咩，嚷嚷嚷嚷
items.translation_en: Baa baa baa baa, shouting shouting
items.description: The delivery of these onomatopoeic sounds utilizes the soft, melodic tones characteristic of the 'Soft Suzhou Dialect' style.

items.title: 苏州大小姐の拟声词挑战~💗
items.transcript: 淙淙淙淙，喳喳喳喳
items.translation_en: Gurgle gurgle, chirp chirp chirp chirp
items.description: The speaker uses the phonetic lightness typical of Wu Chinese (Suzhou region) to articulate these repetitive sounds.

items.title: 苏州大小姐の拟声词挑战~💗
items.transcript: 嚷嚷淙咩喳喳咩
items.translation_en: Shouting gurgle baa chirp chirp baa
items.description: The rhythmic combination of these sounds highlights the specific 'sweet' and soft vocal profile associated with Suzhou speakers.

items.title: 苏州大小姐の拟声词挑战~💗
items.transcript: 咩咩淙淙喳喳嚷嚷
items.translation_en: Baa baa gurgle gurgle chirp chirp shouting
items.description: The fast-paced recitation demonstrates the regional accent's influence on standard onomatopoeic expressions.

items.title: 苏州大小姐の拟声词挑战~💗
items.transcript: 淙淙嚷嚷咩喳淙嚷
items.translation_en: Gurgle gurgle shouting baa chirp gurgle shouting
items.description: The final variation of the chant continues to emphasize the distinctive Suzhou 'Dajiaojie' (young lady) persona through its soft articulation.

items.title: 河北小伙伴猜猜看！石家庄方言da lao zhua、衡水方言bu qi xi、保定方言deng shi、秦皇岛方言bu da yao分别是什么意思？
items.transcript: 快看这树上，怎么有这么多的哒老抓？
items.translation_en: Look at this tree, how come there are so many sparrows?
items.description: Uses the Shijiazhuang dialect term 'da lao zhua' for sparrows.

items.title: 河北小伙伴猜猜看！石家庄方言da lao zhua、衡水方言bu qi xi、保定方言deng shi、秦皇岛方言bu da yao分别是什么意思？
items.transcript: 都到了晌午了，你家小猪咋还不齐心呢？
items.translation_en: It's already noon, why hasn't your little pig eaten anything yet?
items.description: Uses the Hengshui dialect term 'bu qi xi' which colloquially means 'not eating' or 'lacking appetite'.

items.title: 河北小伙伴猜猜看！石家庄方言da lao zhua、衡水方言bu qi xi、保定方言deng shi、秦皇岛方言bu da yao分别是什么意思？
items.transcript: 我们登时就要出发。
items.translation_en: We are leaving right away.
items.description: Uses the Baoding dialect term 'deng shi' meaning immediately or at once.

items.title: 河北小伙伴猜猜看！石家庄方言da lao zhua、衡水方言bu qi xi、保定方言deng shi、秦皇岛方言bu da yao分别是什么意思？
items.transcript: 你这个人，太不打腰了。
items.translation_en: You, as a person, are far too unreliable.
items.description: Uses the Qinhuangdao dialect term 'bu da yao' which means unreliable or untrustworthy.

items.title: 河北小伙伴猜猜看！石家庄方言da lao zhua、衡水方言bu qi xi、保定方言deng shi、秦皇岛方言bu da yao分别是什么意思？
items.transcript: 老板，这个抛多少钱？
items.translation_en: Boss, how much is this pomelo?
items.description: Uses the Fuzhou dialect term 'pao' for pomelo.

items.title: 洛阳客家之源纪念馆讲解员解读洛阳话和客家话发音，“这些无改的乡音，便是我们之间连接的密码。”
items.transcript: 一、二、三、四、五、六、七、八、九、十
items.translation_en: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten
items.description: The speaker demonstrates the Hakka pronunciation of numbers, which differs significantly from Standard Mandarin.

items.title: 洛阳客家之源纪念馆讲解员解读洛阳话和客家话发音，“这些无改的乡音，便是我们之间连接的密码。”
items.transcript: 幺、两、三、唆、五、落、出、八、九、说
items.translation_en: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten
items.description: The speaker demonstrates the Luoyang dialect pronunciation of numbers, showing tonal and phonetic shifts.

items.title: 洛阳客家之源纪念馆讲解员解读洛阳话和客家话发音，“这些无改的乡音，便是我们之间连接的密码。”
items.transcript: 太阳的方言叫日头
items.translation_en: The dialect term for sun is 'ritou'
items.description: Uses the archaic/regional term 'ritou' (sun) common in Hakka and Central Plains dialects.

items.title: 洛阳客家之源纪念馆讲解员解读洛阳话和客家话发音，“这些无改的乡音，便是我们之间连接的密码。”
items.transcript: 水开了就是水滚了
items.translation_en: Water boiling is 'shui gunle'
items.description: Uses the term 'gun' (rolling/boiling) which is a characteristic word choice in Southern and Central Plains dialects compared to standard 'kai'.

items.title: 2012嘉善人民广播电台 ---- 跟我学说嘉善话
items.transcript: 今朝吃得何个呀？
items.translation_en: What did you eat today?
items.description: Uses '今朝' (Jin zao) for 'today' and the characteristic Wu question particle '何个' (ha ge).

items.title: 2012嘉善人民广播电台 ---- 跟我学说嘉善话
items.transcript: 阿奴
items.translation_en: I / Me
items.description: A specific first-person pronoun '阿奴' (a nu) commonly used in the Jiashan area.

items.title: 2012嘉善人民广播电台 ---- 跟我学说嘉善话
items.transcript: 今朝夜里向到何里去吃夜饭呀？
items.translation_en: Where shall we go to have dinner tonight?
items.description: Features several dialectal terms: '夜里向' (evening) and '何里' (where), along with Wu sentence structure.

items.title: 2012嘉善人民广播电台 ---- 跟我学说嘉善话
items.transcript: 饭吃哩口伐？
items.translation_en: Have you eaten?
items.description: A classic Wu dialect greeting using the 'va' question particle typical of northern Wu regions.

items.title: 2012嘉善人民广播电台 ---- 跟我学说嘉善话
items.transcript: 呣真个蛮喜欢侬。
items.translation_en: I really like you quite a bit.
items.description: Demonstrates the 'm' (I/me) and 'nong' (you) pronouns and the 'zhen ge' (really) intensifier unique to this dialect.

items.title: 山东威海小学生第一视角
items.transcript: 这个寒假打算多去几个城市，带大家一待看看，我的大好河山，到底有多美。
items.translation_en: This winter vacation I plan to visit a few more cities, take everyone to have a look, and see how beautiful our great rivers and mountains are.
items.description: Uses '打三' for '打算' and '一待' for '一点/一块', with characteristic Weihai tones where '河山' sounds like '和善'.

items.title: 山东威海小学生第一视角
items.transcript: 目的地上海已经滴达，好气派啊，这是磨呀？
items.translation_en: The destination Shanghai has been reached. It's so grand, what is this?
items.description: Uses '滴达' instead of '抵达' (reached) and '磨' for '什么' (what), which are classic Weihai dialect markers.

items.title: 山东威海小学生第一视角
items.transcript: 哇，这多模民智，在电视看多次东方明珠，从来没这种近距离看过。
items.translation_en: Wow, this is the Oriental Pearl Tower. I've seen it many times on TV, but never from this close.
items.description: The pronunciation of '东方明珠' (Oriental Pearl) as '多模民智' is a distinct dialectal transformation.

items.title: 山东威海小学生第一视角
items.transcript: 这是个船呐？我还以为这是个楼呢。
items.translation_en: Is this a ship? I thought it was a building.
items.description: Pronounces '是个' as '死个' and '楼' with a specific local rising tone common in Eastern Shandong.

items.title: 山东威海小学生第一视角
items.transcript: 上海很好，下次还要来，感谢大家的观直，拜拜。
items.translation_en: Shanghai is great, I'll come back next time. Thanks for following, bye-bye.
items.description: Pronounces '关注' (follow) as '观直', a common phonetic shift in the Weihai region.

items.title: 杭州话“潮汼汼”
items.transcript: 阿国，那点么子量桑，转么处地个量桑，灭虚正把说拉说。
items.translation_en: A-Guo, look at this weather, it's been so damp and sticky lately.
items.description: Includes Hangzhou-specific vocabulary like '量桑' (weather/time) and '灭虚正' (sticky/greasy), along with Wu Chinese phonology.

items.title: 杭州话“潮汼汼”
items.transcript: 我心哈总觉得处斤恰，潮汼汼的。
items.translation_en: I always feel damp and sticky, very humid.
items.description: Features the target dialect term '潮汼汼' (cháo niū niū) and '处斤恰' (sticky/damp).

items.title: 杭州话“潮汼汼”
items.transcript: 塞故意处斤恰热呼呼的蛮苏胡的。
items.translation_en: It's intentionally damp and warm, quite comfortable.
items.description: Uses the dialectal pronunciation '苏胡' for '舒服' (comfortable) and the local adverb '塞故意'.

items.title: 杭州话“潮汼汼”
items.transcript: 我也看开外啊，我片子都看开。我均里想外的，咋啦？
items.translation_en: I've seen it too, I've watched all the films. I'm just thinking about it, what's wrong?
items.description: Uses the '开' (kāi) aspect marker, which is a distinctive feature of the Hangzhou dialect compared to Mandarin.

items.title: 杭州话“潮汼汼”
items.transcript: 居管那个的事体了拔。奥，难怪子嘞早朗来还忙什懂得帮麻。
items.translation_en: It's about that matter. Oh, no wonder you were busy helping out early this morning.
items.description: Contains '事体' (matter) and '早朗' (morning), which are typical Wu/Hangzhou dialect nouns and time markers.

items.title: 耍阔子系列(一):肥肉抹嘴装有钱。荔浦人叫学洋为耍阔子，贫穷装有钱。#荔浦话词典 #耍阔子 #学洋 #桂林阔子
items.transcript: 荔浦有个十月横到学样，爱到耍阔子。
items.translation_en: In Lipu, there is a story about Xueyang, who loved to show off his wealth.
items.description: Uses the specific local dialect term 'Shua Kuozi' and dialectal pronunciation for 'Xueyang'.

items.title: 耍阔子系列(一):肥肉抹嘴装有钱。荔浦人叫学洋为耍阔子，贫穷装有钱。#荔浦话词典 #耍阔子 #学洋 #桂林阔子
items.transcript: 什么叫耍阔子呢？全桂林人喊管顶阔子。
items.translation_en: What does 'Shua Kuozi' mean? People across Guilin call it 'Guandong Kuozi'.
items.description: Introduces the regional synonym 'Guandong Kuozi' used in Guilin dialect.

items.title: 耍阔子系列(一):肥肉抹嘴装有钱。荔浦人叫学洋为耍阔子，贫穷装有钱。#荔浦话词典 #耍阔子 #学洋 #桂林阔子
items.transcript: 就是没得钱装有钱呢。我们就爱讲喊到管顶阔子。
items.translation_en: It means having no money but pretending to have it. We like to call it 'Guandong Kuozi'.
items.description: Includes dialectal 'Mei de' for 'don't have' and colloquial sentence structures.

items.title: 耍阔子系列(一):肥肉抹嘴装有钱。荔浦人叫学洋为耍阔子，贫穷装有钱。#荔浦话词典 #耍阔子 #学洋 #桂林阔子
items.transcript: 这个学样原本好穷呢，但是他细波饭中爱装，天天有大鱼大肉吃的。
items.translation_en: This Xueyang was originally very poor, but he loved to show off when eating, as if he had big fish and meat every day.
items.description: Contains 'Xi bo fan' which is a local dialect expression related to eating meals.

items.title: 耍阔子系列(一):肥肉抹嘴装有钱。荔浦人叫学洋为耍阔子，贫穷装有钱。#荔浦话词典 #耍阔子 #学洋 #桂林阔子
items.transcript: 学样们呢？吃饱了没？你看你嘴巴的油还有抹客。
items.translation_en: Hey Xueyang? Are you full? Look, you've still got grease smeared on your mouth.
items.description: Features the dialectal sentence particle 'men ne' and the specific local word 'ma ke' (smeared/remnant).

items.title: 兰州方言电影教学之虎口脱险part Ⅰ（2）
items.transcript: 哎，天呐！实在不好意思啊。真细，发不这个行客。
items.translation_en: Oh my god! I'm so sorry. Truly, I can't handle this guy.
items.description: The term '行客' (heng ke) is used as a dialectal way to refer to a person or customer, and the overall intonation is distinctively Lanzhou.

items.title: 兰州方言电影教学之虎口脱险part Ⅰ（2）
items.transcript: 嘿，咋爬上去，爬上去。
items.translation_en: Hey, how do you climb up, climb up.
items.description: The use of '咋' (za) instead of '怎么' (zenme) combined with the specific Northwestern vowel shifts is characteristic of the Lanzhou region.

items.title: 兰州方言电影教学之虎口脱险part Ⅰ（2）
items.transcript: 哎呀，这哈，这下把得消命拔了。
items.translation_en: Oh no, this time, this time I'm finished.
items.description: '这哈' (zhe ha) is a quintessential Lanzhou dialect term used to mean 'now' or 'this time'.

items.title: 兰州方言电影教学之虎口脱险part Ⅰ（2）
items.transcript: 哎，哎呀，你咋落在这个地方了撒。
items.translation_en: Oh dear, how did you end up in this place?
items.description: The ending particle '撒' (sa) and the use of '咋' (za) are highly indicative of Gansu/Lanzhou regional speech.

items.title: 兰州方言电影教学之虎口脱险part Ⅰ（2）
items.transcript: 哎哟，板死你。
items.translation_en: Oh my, you are so stubborn/annoying.
items.description: '板死' (ban si) is a local Lanzhou slang expression used to describe someone's behavior or personality in a specific way.

items.title: 给大家科普下，湖北咸宁说的是“韩语”😂
items.transcript: 藕 (ngou)
items.translation_en: Lotus root
items.description: In the Wuhan dialect, the word for lotus root is pronounced with a distinct nasal 'ng' initial, differing from the standard Mandarin 'ou'.

items.title: 给大家科普下，湖北咸宁说的是“韩语”😂
items.transcript: 藕 (niao)
items.translation_en: Lotus root
items.description: A speaker from a different Hubei region pronounces 'lotus root' as 'niao', illustrating internal dialectal variation within the province.

items.title: 给大家科普下，湖北咸宁说的是“韩语”😂
items.transcript: 鸟 (jodt)
items.translation_en: Bird
items.description: The Xianning dialect pronunciation of 'bird' sounds like 'jodt' or 'jods', which is phonetically very distinct from Mandarin and is the focus of the 'Korean' comparison.

items.title: 河南郑州12区县方言测试，看看能听懂几个
items.transcript: 那儿也不给整周了小闺女儿太咧那都不着，啥叫撒娇啥叫温柔
items.translation_en: That's not proper, little girl, you're being too much, that won't do. What is acting spoiled? What is being gentle?
items.description: Uses '不给整周' (not proper/not right) and '不着' (no/not okay), which are idiomatic expressions in the Zhongyuan area of Zhengzhou.

items.title: 河南郑州12区县方言测试，看看能听懂几个
items.transcript: 啥叫压结，都照吧？啥叫逗们，也都照吧？
items.translation_en: What does 'yajie' mean? Is it all okay? What does 'doumen' mean? Is that also okay?
items.description: Includes dialect words '压结' (evening), '逗们' (joke/tease), and '照' (okay/fine), typical of the Erqi district accent.

items.title: 河南郑州12区县方言测试，看看能听懂几个
items.transcript: 呀，下雪了，地上光滑溜溜，弄不好摔个杨白叉。
items.translation_en: Oh, it's snowing, the ground is so slippery, you might end up falling flat on your back.
items.description: '杨白叉' is a specific regional slang for falling over with limbs splayed, common in the Jinshui district.

items.title: 河南郑州12区县方言测试，看看能听懂几个
items.transcript: 你个昂人站那儿啥？可懒样扎。
items.translation_en: Why are you standing there like that, you annoying person? So lazy.
items.description: Features the term '昂人' (annoying/hateful) and the unique phonetic phrasing of the Shangjie district dialect.

items.title: 河南郑州12区县方言测试，看看能听懂几个
items.transcript: 你咋整哥一任了？你要再这好样，老子一锤削死你。
items.translation_en: Why are you acting so annoying? If you keep doing this, I'll hit you.
items.description: Uses local verbs like '削' (hit) and the phrase '哥一任' (annoying) in a thick Xingyang regional accent.

items.title: 江苏扬州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 我跟你回扬州，怕你吃不惯这里的菜...最后扬州大肥中...扬州的小待，长得秋...
items.translation_en: I'll take you back to Yangzhou, fearing you won't like the food here... Yangzhou's large 'fat seeds' (local term)... Yangzhou girls are pretty...
items.description: Uses intense Yangzhou urban dialect (Guangling) in a rhythmic rap style with local slang like 'Xiao Dai' (girls).

items.title: 江苏扬州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 你说怎么好吃法？我吃过这个瘦肉面你就快...
items.translation_en: How would you describe the taste? I've had this lean meat noodle and you should hurry...
items.description: Features the softer, more melodic 'soft' dialect of the Hanjiang District.

items.title: 江苏扬州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 他妈妈说酸，酸，酸...刚才这个话恐怕只有扬州人听得懂，这个酸就是大蒜的意思。
items.translation_en: His mother said 'suan, suan, suan'... only Yangzhou people likely understand this; 'suan' here means garlic.
items.description: Explicitly discusses a lexical variation in the Jiangdu dialect where 'suan' (sour) refers to garlic.

items.title: 江苏扬州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 你看那个姑里，那个嘎子...你看你那个子弯皮相的样子。
items.translation_en: Look at that girl, look at that... look at your naughty/mischievous appearance.
items.description: Demonstrates the distinct phonology of the Baoying dialect, which differs significantly from the urban Yangzhou accent.

items.title: 江苏扬州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 有一种吃饭叫多桑子，有一种睡觉叫挺尸。
items.translation_en: There's a way of saying 'eating' called 'duo sang zi', and 'sleeping' is called 'ting shi'.
items.description: Directly lists Yizheng-specific slang and colorful local expressions.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路露营，方言聊天
items.transcript: 你这交活的神呀，你这，你这多重？
items.translation_en: You're really something, how heavy is this?
items.description: The speaker uses the local expression '交活的神' (amazing/extraordinary) with heavy Guanzhong intonation and characteristic Shaanxi vowel shifts.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路露营，方言聊天
items.transcript: 你是哪里的？哪里的？哪里的？
items.translation_en: Where are you from?
items.description: The pronunciation of '哪里的' (nali di) and the repetitive questioning style are typical of local Shaanxi conversational habits.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路露营，方言聊天
items.transcript: 那你上班，你一跑跑出去三个月上班？
items.translation_en: So you have a job, yet you're out traveling for three months while working?
items.description: The speaker's tones on '上班' (work) and '三个月' (three months) follow the tonal patterns of the Xianyang sub-dialect of Guanzhong Mandarin.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路露营，方言聊天
items.transcript: 叫我去我可不去。
items.translation_en: I wouldn't go even if you asked me.
items.description: The rhythm and the specific downward inflection on the final negation are classic features of the Shaanxi dialect.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路露营，方言聊天
items.transcript: 就你个人，你也莫造个伴？
items.translation_en: Just you alone, you didn't find a partner?
items.description: Uses '个人' (oneself) and the dialectal '莫' (no/not) or '造' (find/make) characteristic of local northwestern speech.

items.title: 福建漳州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 漳州... 吃饭叫呷崩, 喝粥叫呷每, 吃面叫呷米, 钱叫luo, 很好叫liho.
items.translation_en: In Zhangzhou... eating rice is called 'jia beng', drinking porridge is 'jia mei', eating noodles is 'jia mi', money is 'luo', and very good is 'liho'.
items.description: This segment explicitly translates common Mandarin phrases into the specific phonology and vocabulary of the Dongshan dialect.

items.title: 福建漳州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 我们这边吃饭不叫吃饭, 叫呷崩, 然后睡觉不叫睡觉, 叫困, 竹笋不叫竹笋, 叫笋呐, 然后猪蹄不叫猪蹄, 叫低滴.
items.translation_en: Here, eating rice isn't called 'chifan', it's 'jia beng'. Sleeping isn't 'shuijiao', it's 'kun'. Bamboo shoots aren't 'zhusun', they're 'sun na'. Pig trotters aren't 'zhuti', they're 'di di'.
items.description: The speaker contrasts standard Mandarin terms with the Longwen district dialect counterparts for everyday items and actions.

items.title: 福建漳州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 早起达保红, 就系杠情三逆. 你若不欢喜, 陪陪向拉都公姆滴.
items.translation_en: Waking up early... (local slang). If you're not happy, (colloquial expression).
items.description: Features the distinct rhythmic patterns and specific colloquial expressions of the Xiangcheng district dialect.

items.title: 福建漳州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 漳浦人, 漳浦话, 漳浦阿嘛呀真听话. 漳浦人, 个个动, 做朋友, 妥妥当.
items.translation_en: Zhangpu people, Zhangpu dialect, Zhangpu girls are really nice. Zhangpu people are active, as friends, they are reliable.
items.description: This is a rhyming chant delivered in the Zhangpu dialect, emphasizing local identity and specific vowel shifts.

items.title: 福建漳州，哪里女生说话最好听
items.transcript: 我今天跟你涂漆一下, 查某要取哪里的? 是要取我们漳州诏安的. 因为诏安的女孩子水, 又贴安.
items.translation_en: I'll tell you today, where should you find a wife? You should find one in Zhao'an, Zhangzhou. Because Zhao'an girls are beautiful and gentle.
items.description: Uses specific Minnan dialect words like '查某' (woman/wife) and '水' (beautiful) common in the Zhao'an region.

items.title: 【学藏语】真正的零基础从小白到精通保姆级教程-第20课
items.transcript: ga u pha, gup gup gup gup gup
items.translation_en: ga u pha, gup gup gup gup gup
items.description: Demonstration of the Lhasa Tibetan pronunciation for the combination of consonant 'ga', vowel 'u', and suffix 'pha'.

items.title: 【学藏语】真正的零基础从小白到精通保姆级教程-第20课
items.transcript: ja u pha, jup jup jup jup jup
items.translation_en: ja u pha, jup jup jup jup jup
items.description: Demonstration of the Lhasa Tibetan pronunciation for the combination of consonant 'ja', vowel 'u', and suffix 'pha'.

items.title: 【学藏语】真正的零基础从小白到精通保姆级教程-第20课
items.transcript: da u pha, dup dup dup dup dup
items.translation_en: da u pha, dup dup dup dup dup
items.description: Demonstration of the Lhasa Tibetan pronunciation for the combination of consonant 'da', vowel 'u', and suffix 'pha'.

items.title: 【学藏语】真正的零基础从小白到精通保姆级教程-第20课
items.transcript: ba u pha, bup bup bup bup bup
items.translation_en: ba u pha, bup bup bup bup bup
items.description: Demonstration of the Lhasa Tibetan pronunciation for the combination of consonant 'ba', vowel 'u', and suffix 'pha'.

items.title: 【学藏语】真正的零基础从小白到精通保姆级教程-第20课
items.transcript: za u pha, zup zup zup zup zup
items.translation_en: za u pha, zup zup zup zup zup
items.description: Demonstration of the Lhasa Tibetan pronunciation for the combination of consonant 'za', vowel 'u', and suffix 'pha'.

items.title: 撒西不理哒呐，不敢带你回浦江
items.transcript: 不知道那浦江，怪色我来个地
items.translation_en: Don't you know Pujiang? It's such a great place.
items.description: The speaker uses the Pujiang dialect particle '怪色我来' (guaise wo lai) which acts as an intensifier similar to 'very' or 'truly' in standard Mandarin.

items.title: 撒西不理哒呐，不敢带你回浦江
items.transcript: 浦江姑娘特漂亮
items.translation_en: Pujiang girls are exceptionally beautiful.
items.description: The pronunciation of 'Pujiang' and the adjective 'beautiful' follows the specific tonal and vowel patterns of the Jinhua/Pujiang branch of Wu Chinese.

items.title: 山西晋中市暴雪，上一次榆次这么大的雪还是二十多年前了，原音直出。
items.transcript: 这雪下得真厚嘞。
items.translation_en: This snow is falling so thick.
items.description: The sentence ends with the particle 'lei' (嘞), which is characteristic of the Jinzhong dialect, along with a distinct rising-falling tone pattern.

items.title: 山西晋中市暴雪，上一次榆次这么大的雪还是二十多年前了，原音直出。
items.transcript: 这应该是，应该是榆次的小雪杠杠。
items.translation_en: This should be, should be the Yuci's snow mounds.
items.description: The speaker uses the local term 'Gangan' (杠杠) and the pronunciation of 'Yuci' reflects the local Jin Chinese accent.

items.title: 山西晋中市暴雪，上一次榆次这么大的雪还是二十多年前了，原音直出。
items.transcript: 最起码灯光要全特勒。
items.translation_en: At least the lights should be fully visible.
items.description: The phrase 'quan te le' is a dialectal expression in the Shanxi/Jinzhong region used to describe something being distinct, prominent, or fully visible.

items.title: 山西晋中市暴雪，上一次榆次这么大的雪还是二十多年前了，原音直出。
items.transcript: 这交通接太坏了，简直跟东北一样清不下的雪。
items.translation_en: The traffic is just too bad; it's just like the Northeast where the snow never stops.
items.description: The speaker uses 'jie' as an intensifier and the phrase 'qing bu xia' (清不下) to describe non-stop snow, both typical of the local vernacular.

items.title: 山西晋中市暴雪，上一次榆次这么大的雪还是二十多年前了，原音直出。
items.transcript: 怪不得人家东北冬天就是筒儿两下子。
items.translation_en: No wonder people in the Northeast are just used to it in winter.
items.description: The use of 'Guaibude' with a specific local accent and the colloquialism 'tongr liangxiazi' indicates a regional dialectal influence.

items.title: 爱讲武汉话的朵拉之朵拉煨藕汤
items.transcript: 十来个单位排队煨藕汤啊，好香啊
items.translation_en: Ten units are queuing up for slow-cooked lotus root soup, it smells so good.
items.description: Uses the specific Hubei cooking term '煨' (slow-simmer) and exhibits characteristic Wuhan tonal shifts.

items.title: 爱讲武汉话的朵拉之朵拉煨藕汤
items.transcript: 帮我煨粉的，不粉我不吃的，要像那个香蕉一样粉
items.translation_en: Simmer it until it's powdery; if it's not powdery I won't eat it, it must be as powdery as a banana.
items.description: The word '粉' (fěn) describes the starchy, powdery texture highly prized in Wuhan lotus root, spoken with a local accent.

items.title: 爱讲武汉话的朵拉之朵拉煨藕汤
items.transcript: 你个狗要求还蛮多的，有的吃就不错了
items.translation_en: You dog, you have quite a lot of demands; be glad you have something to eat.
items.description: Uses '蛮' (very/quite) and local slang/intonation common in Wuhan street speech.

items.title: 爱讲武汉话的朵拉之朵拉煨藕汤
items.transcript: 盖本地黑芝麻酱，我都恨不得几个要晕了
items.translation_en: With this local black sesame paste, I feel like I'm about to faint.
items.description: Mentions local ingredients with the distinct rising and falling tones of the Wuhan dialect.

items.title: 爱讲武汉话的朵拉之朵拉煨藕汤
items.transcript: 排骨藕汤、香蕉、煎饺、芝麻酱、蛋糕、做成了
items.translation_en: Rib lotus root soup, bananas, fried dumplings, sesame paste, cake—it's all finished.
items.description: A list of food items delivered with the specific rhythmic cadence and phonology of Wuhan speech.

items.title: 遵义3区9县2市女生，哪里说话最好听
items.transcript: 老子牙齿巴不求罗，你家马儿。
items.translation_en: My teeth are falling out/worthless, you clown.
items.description: Uses Bozhou-specific slang '不求罗' (a common local negation or dismissal) and '家马儿' (a local derogatory term for a fool or clown).

items.title: 遵义3区9县2市女生，哪里说话最好听
items.transcript: 好丁我送你到门口，打个伞等我罗家婆。
items.translation_en: Let me walk you to the door; hold an umbrella for me, my wife.
items.description: Features distinctive Suiyang County phonetics and vocabulary like '罗家婆' (local term for wife) and the specific 'o' vowel shifts typical of Southwestern Mandarin in Guizhou.

items.title: 遵义3区9县2市女生，哪里说话最好听
items.transcript: 凤冈吃早饭叫过早，吃午饭叫吃早午，吃晚饭叫吃夜饭，长得好看叫做趁头。
items.translation_en: In Fenggang, breakfast is called 'guozao', lunch is 'chizaowu', dinner is 'chiyefan', and looking good is called 'chentou'.
items.description: Explicitly lists Fenggang-specific vocabulary for meals and appearance, which differs from Standard Mandarin.

items.title: 遵义3区9县2市女生，哪里说话最好听
items.transcript: 看看哪些湄潭人还晓得这些湄潭的土话不：光八丁点，古道劲。
items.translation_en: Let's see which Meitan people still know these local Meitan phrases: 'guang ba ding dian' (naked/exposed), 'gu dao jin'.
items.description: The speaker explicitly references 'local slang' (土话) and provides specific dialect examples like '光八丁点'.

items.title: 遵义3区9县2市女生，哪里说话最好听
items.transcript: 哥哥，来喝板板茶了，赤水的板板茶好喝得。
items.translation_en: Brother, come drink some Banban tea; Chishui's Banban tea is delicious.
items.description: Includes a cultural-linguistic reference to '板板茶', a traditional tea culture specific to Chishui, and uses the local suffix '得' for emphasis.

items.title: 泰州人教徐州人说河南话？
items.transcript: 一把就散。对，一把就散，一把就散。一把就散。
items.translation_en: Scattered with one grab. Yes, scattered with one grab, scattered with one grab. Scattered with one grab.
items.description: The phrase is used as a mnemonic to mimic the distinct third and fourth tone variations characteristic of the Henan/Central Plains dialect pronunciation of '1893'.

items.title: 泰州人教徐州人说河南话？
items.transcript: 一把就散，这就是河南话，标准河南话。这就是标准。
items.translation_en: Scattered with one grab, that's Henan dialect, standard Henan dialect. That is the standard.
items.description: The speaker explicitly identifies the phonetic style as 'standard Henan dialect' while repeating the dialect-inflected mnemonic phrase.

items.title: 馆长10.30直播③：馆长到达断桥，学习丹东方言，好有意思哦
items.transcript: 八苦八苦是什么意思？就是苦，特别苦。麻爪是什么意思？就是懵住了。
items.translation_en: What does 'Baku' mean? It means bitter, very bitter. What does 'Mazhua' mean? It means being stunned or confused.
items.description: Explains local Dandong terms '八苦' (baku) and '麻爪' (mazhua).

items.title: 馆长10.30直播③：馆长到达断桥，学习丹东方言，好有意思哦
items.transcript: 丹东是个带一亲的城市，带一亲就是讨人喜欢。
items.translation_en: Dandong is a 'daiyiqin' city. 'Daiyiqin' means lovable or likeable.
items.description: Introduction of the specific Dandong dialect term '带一亲' (daiyiqin).

items.title: 馆长10.30直播③：馆长到达断桥，学习丹东方言，好有意思哦
items.transcript: 你咋耍猫烂赖。
items.translation_en: Why are you acting up/being unreasonable?
items.description: A local slang phrase '耍猫烂赖' (shua mao lan lai) used to describe someone acting unreasonably.

items.title: 馆长10.30直播③：馆长到达断桥，学习丹东方言，好有意思哦
items.transcript: 这大海鲜滋滋鲜的。
items.translation_en: This seafood is extremely fresh.
items.description: Uses the dialectal intensifier '滋滋鲜' (zizi xian) to describe extreme freshness.

items.title: 馆长10.30直播③：馆长到达断桥，学习丹东方言，好有意思哦
items.transcript: 那嘎嘎鲜。草莓刮刮了，刮刮了是好，顶嘴尖儿了。
items.translation_en: That is extremely fresh. The strawberries are top-tier. 'Guagua' means good, the very best.
items.description: Uses '嘎嘎' (gaga) and '刮刮了' (guagua le), both common in Northeastern and Dandong dialects as intensifiers and markers of quality.

items.title: 温州第一公主殿下
items.transcript: 世界第一公主殿下
items.translation_en: World's number one princess
items.description: The pronunciation 'si-ka-di-i-gung-tsu-di-o' is characteristic of the Wenzhou dialect's unique phonology, which differs significantly from Mandarin.

items.title: 温州第一公主殿下
items.transcript: 要那样，带那好去，好去，好去
items.translation_en: Need to be like that, take it there, there, there
items.description: The vocalist uses Wenzhou-specific grammatical structures and tonal variations typical of Southern Wu dialects.

items.title: 安徽芜湖市7县市区方言，你能听懂几个?
items.transcript: 跟着老弟你们青说，你们芜湖市都会过日子
items.translation_en: Listen to me, brother; you people in Wuhu really know how to live your lives.
items.description: Uses '青说' (qing shuo) and specific local intonation patterns unique to the Wanzhi District area.

items.title: 安徽芜湖市7县市区方言，你能听懂几个?
items.transcript: 给死这燕迷马呼勒，结看你色它形色，在外面切它形亏
items.translation_en: It's extremely blurry/confusing. Look at how you're acting; you'll suffer for it outside.
items.description: Features the highly localized Nanling idiom '燕迷马呼' (yan mi ma hu) to describe a state of confusion or blurriness.

items.title: 安徽芜湖市7县市区方言，你能听懂几个?
items.transcript: 我是土生土长的无为人，救我无为话，是真能等
items.translation_en: I am a native of Wuwei; my Wuwei dialect is truly something else.
items.description: The speaker uses the Wuwei-specific expression '真能等' (zhen neng deng) to mean something is impressive or unique.

items.title: 安徽芜湖市7县市区方言，你能听懂几个?
items.transcript: 真的你真三更，咋那火化子，你干几花你拉不南宛了
items.translation_en: Really, it's so late, why are you so restless? What are you doing?
items.description: Includes '火化子' (huo hua zi), a specific Fanchang dialect term describing a person who is hyperactive or noisy.

items.title: 安徽芜湖市7县市区方言，你能听懂几个?
items.transcript: 好看叫遮欠子，假的叫遮诈丹，大胆叫灯破子
items.translation_en: Good-looking is called 'zhe qian zi', fake is called 'zhe zha dan', and bold is called 'deng po zi'.
items.description: The segment explicitly lists specific Jiujiang District dialect synonyms that differ significantly from standard Mandarin.

items.title: 【生灵重塑】崩溃到说重庆话！
items.transcript: 老子也是... 袍哥儿快还是你嘴到抽了。我真死嘞。
items.translation_en: I'm also... Paoge... my mouth is twitching. I'm really dying.
items.description: The speaker uses 'Laozi' (a coarse self-reference) and 'Paoge' (a term for members of historical Sichuan/Chongqing secret societies), both central to regional identity and slang.

items.title: 【生灵重塑】崩溃到说重庆话！
items.transcript: 老子，老子，老子，老子！快跑，小小滴脑子！
items.translation_en: Me, me, me, me! Run fast, tiny brain!
items.description: Repeated use of 'Laozi' with the aggressive, emphatic intonation typical of the Chongqing dialect during high emotional states.

items.title: 【生灵重塑】崩溃到说重庆话！
items.transcript: 小小滴脑子。
items.translation_en: Tiny brain.
items.description: The pronunciation of '脑子' (naozi) exhibits the nasal-lateral (n/l) variation common in Southwestern Mandarin dialects.

items.title: 中国最难懂吉林方言，通化延边话风趣幽默，长春辽源话完全听不懂
items.transcript: 瞧你还不省心啊，你再瞅一瞅。
items.translation_en: Look at you, still making me worry; take another look.
items.description: Uses the word '瞅' (chǒu), which is the standard dialect term for 'to look' in Jilin and Northeast China.

items.title: 中国最难懂吉林方言，通化延边话风趣幽默，长春辽源话完全听不懂
items.transcript: 肩膀头子，胯窝轴子，波棱盖儿，嘎巴拉子。
items.translation_en: Shoulder, hip joint, kneecap, ankle bone.
items.description: Features a string of specific anatomical terms unique to Northeast Mandarin dialects.

items.title: 中国最难懂吉林方言，通化延边话风趣幽默，长春辽源话完全听不懂
items.transcript: 啥是啥？
items.translation_en: What is what?
items.description: Uses '啥' (shá) as a dialectal substitute for the standard '什么' (shénme), used here in a rhythmic, local context.

items.title: 中国最难懂吉林方言，通化延边话风趣幽默，长春辽源话完全听不懂
items.transcript: 卡秃噜皮了。
items.translation_en: I scraped my skin off.
items.description: The phrase '卡秃噜皮' (qiǎ tū lu pí) is a highly specific and iconic Jilin/Northeast idiom for skinning or grazing one's skin.

items.title: 中国最难懂吉林方言，通化延边话风趣幽默，长春辽源话完全听不懂
items.transcript: 二白，辽源市方言... 是一直简单并且直接表达意思。
items.translation_en: 'Er bai' is a Liaoyuan dialect term... it refers to expressing meaning simply and directly.
items.description: Directly identifies and explains a specific local term ('二白') from the Liaoyuan region of Jilin.

items.title: 青海西宁方言朗诵《出师表》
items.transcript: 先帝创业未半而中道崩殂
items.translation_en: The late Emperor was cut off in the midst of his great work.
items.description: The pronunciation of 'chuang' and 'zhong' features the characteristically deep and flat tones of the Lanzhou-Xining Mandarin subgroup.

items.title: 青海西宁方言朗诵《出师表》
items.transcript: 然侍卫之臣不懈于内，忠志之士忘身于外者
items.translation_en: Yet the ministers of the Guard are untiring within the court, and the loyal soldiers are self-forgetful without.
items.description: Vowel shifts in 'wei' and 'nei' are indicative of the local Qinghai phonetic system.

items.title: 青海西宁方言朗诵《出师表》
items.transcript: 宫中府中，俱为一体；陟罚臧否，不宜异同
items.translation_en: In the Palace and in the Headquarters, there must be one spirit; the promotion of the good and the punishment of the evil should not differ.
items.description: The nasal endings in 'gong' and 'zhong' are pronounced with a specific regional resonance unique to the Xining area.

items.title: 青海西宁方言朗诵《出师表》
items.transcript: 将军向宠，性行淑均，晓畅军事
items.translation_en: General Xiang Chong is a man of nature and conduct both fair and just, and he is well versed in military matters.
items.description: The initial consonants in 'jun' and 'shi' display the retroflex/non-retroflex patterns typical of Northwest dialects.

items.title: 青海西宁方言朗诵《出师表》
items.transcript: 愿陛下托臣以讨贼兴复之效
items.translation_en: I pray that Your Majesty will entrust me with the task of punishing the rebels and restoring the Han.
items.description: The word 'bi xia' (Your Majesty) is delivered with the rising-falling tone contour specific to the Xining vernacular.

items.title: 都说河北坝上方言最难听懂，与当地人聊天语言毫无障碍。
items.transcript: 你这拖了啥了？
items.translation_en: What are you hauling here?
items.description: The local accent on '啥' and the verb '拖' are characteristic of the northern Hebei region.

items.title: 都说河北坝上方言最难听懂，与当地人聊天语言毫无障碍。
items.transcript: 哟多载啦？
items.translation_en: How many days has it been?
items.description: The use of '载' (zai) as a quantifier for days is a specific feature of Bashang and surrounding northern dialects.

items.title: 都说河北坝上方言最难听懂，与当地人聊天语言毫无障碍。
items.transcript: 出来多少载了？
items.translation_en: How many days have you been out?
items.description: Repeated use of the dialectal time quantifier '载'.

items.title: 都说河北坝上方言最难听懂，与当地人聊天语言毫无障碍。
items.transcript: 今年遭年些，没下到雨，草不着。
items.translation_en: This year is a bad year; there's been no rain, so the grass isn't growing.
items.description: The term '遭年' refers to a drought or bad agricultural year, and '草不着' is a local way of saying the grass isn't flourishing.

items.title: 都说河北坝上方言最难听懂，与当地人聊天语言毫无障碍。
items.transcript: 今年可干了，这牲口可没法弄了。
items.translation_en: It's so dry this year; it's impossible to manage the livestock.
items.description: The phrasing '没法弄了' used with the local accent on '牲口' (livestock) reflects the regional vernacular.

items.title: 谁说山西人只会挖煤？我带着“运城笑话”来炸场！
items.transcript: 我讲脱口秀啊是因为我老家山西运城，是中国笑话之城。
items.translation_en: I do stand-up because my hometown is Yuncheng, Shanxi, which is China's city of jokes.
items.description: Establishes the specific regional identity of Yuncheng and its local cultural branding.

items.title: 谁说山西人只会挖煤？我带着“运城笑话”来炸场！
items.transcript: 握着我的手跟我说，这是老家的段子，你给同事带几个。
items.translation_en: Holding my hand and saying to me, these are jokes from our hometown, take a few for your colleagues.
items.description: Reflects the local cultural quirk of treating 'jokes' like physical specialty goods from the region.

items.title: 谁说山西人只会挖煤？我带着“运城笑话”来炸场！
items.transcript: 大盘鸡、稷山饼子、麻花、闻喜煮饼、新绛火锅、东湖早市、碳水一条街
items.translation_en: Big plate chicken, Jishan pancakes, fried dough twists, Wenxi boiled cakes, Xinjiang hot pot, Donghu morning market, and carb street.
items.description: Features a dense list of hyper-local culinary specialties and locations specific to the Yuncheng area.

items.title: 谁说山西人只会挖煤？我带着“运城笑话”来炸场！
items.transcript: 所以我们喜欢说闲话，希望老乡们都参与进来。
items.translation_en: So we like to tell jokes, I hope my fellow townsfolk will all join in.
items.description: Uses the term '闲话' (xiánhuà), which in local Yuncheng/Shanxi context refers to telling humorous stories or jokes rather than idle gossip.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路闲聊，陕西方言
items.transcript: 娃，你这娃，你这娃，这一两个娃，随便拉娃都是要城里一套房呢。
items.translation_en: Kids, these kids of yours, these one or two kids, any kid you pick up requires a house in the city.
items.description: Frequent use of '娃' (wa) for child and distinct Shaanxi tonal patterns.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路闲聊，陕西方言
items.transcript: 现在人是生了一个，怕生第二个。不敢生，普通人一般都害怕生第二个。
items.translation_en: People nowadays have one, but they're afraid to have a second. They don't dare; ordinary people are generally afraid of having a second child.
items.description: Uses local Shaanxi pronunciation for common verbs and maintains regional sentence structures.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路闲聊，陕西方言
items.transcript: 老了以后，你不管咋么事，老了，你看个病啊，还要往那个35医院去那个。
items.translation_en: When you're old, no matter what happens, when you're old and need to see a doctor, you still have to head to that 35th Hospital.
items.description: Uses '咋么' (zame) instead of '怎么' (zenme) and refers to local Xianyang landmarks in dialect.

items.title: 骑行陕西省，咸阳市，河堤路闲聊，陕西方言
items.transcript: 贫富差距太严重了。咱这一年挣那钱，有的人几年都挣不到。
items.translation_en: The wealth gap is too severe. The money we make in a year, some people can't make in several years.
items.description: The use of '咱' (zan) and the specific cadence of the complaint are characteristic of Guanzhong dialect speakers.

items.title: 徐州人的“滋”#搞笑
items.transcript: 得了工作啦。那可滋咯，自己能挣钱了，不要问家里人要了。
items.translation_en: Got a job. That's pretty smug; you can earn money yourself and don't need to ask your family for it.
items.description: Uses the signature Xuzhou word '滋' (zi) meaning satisfied/smug, along with the characteristic sentence-final particle '咯' (luo).

items.title: 徐州人的“滋”#搞笑
items.transcript: 那更滋咯，天天啥也不要干，也不要早起，也不要晚费。
items.translation_en: That's even more smug; you don't have to do anything every day, no waking up early, no staying up late.
items.description: Uses '更滋' (geng zi) for heightened satisfaction and local phrasing for daily routines.

items.title: 徐州人的“滋”#搞笑
items.transcript: 那可滋咯，以后考个公务员，可不给村里光了。滋死咯。
items.translation_en: That's pretty smug; take the civil service exam later and bring honor to the village. Smug to death.
items.description: Features '滋死咯' (zi si luo), a superlative dialect expression for extreme satisfaction.

items.title: 徐州人的“滋”#搞笑
items.transcript: 那更滋咯，搁某找个拜生，还能给家里分担分担。
items.translation_en: That's even more smug; find a job somewhere else and you can help share the family's burden.
items.description: Uses the Xuzhou dialect term '拜生' (bàisheng) which refers to a job or a way to make a living.

items.title: 徐州人的“滋”#搞笑
items.transcript: 我得管那更滋咯，天天啥也不要干，大事小事都不要操心。滋死咯。
items.translation_en: I'd say that's even more smug; you don't have to do anything every day and don't have to worry about matters big or small. Smug to death.
items.description: Combines several dialect markers including '更滋咯' and the emphatic '滋死咯' with local intonation.

items.title: 天津卫学闽南方言の章度总结
items.transcript: 王先，請坐，欲去陀落？
items.translation_en: Mr. Wang, please sit, where are you going?
items.description: Contains 'tó-lo̍h' (where) and 'siān' (honorific for sir), which are characteristic of Southern Min syntax and vocabulary.

items.title: 天津卫学闽南方言の章度总结
items.transcript: 中山公園喔跳舞的人真多真鬧熱。
items.translation_en: There are many people dancing in Zhongshan Park, it's very lively.
items.description: Uses the term 'lau-jiat' for lively, a specific Hokkien/Min Nan adjective, and 'tsin tsue' for 'very many'.

items.title: 天津卫学闽南方言の章度总结
items.transcript: 許先，你好！欸，士林小姐，真久無看到你。
items.translation_en: Mr. Xu, hello! Oh, Ms. Shilin, long time no see.
items.description: Includes the phrase 'tsin kú bô khòaⁿ-tio̍h' (long time not seen), demonstrating Southern Min negation and aspect markers.

items.title: 天津卫学闽南方言の章度总结
items.transcript: 阿伯、阿姆，恭喜恭喜，我來甲恁拜年。
items.translation_en: Uncle, Auntie, congratulations, I've come to pay you a New Year call.
items.description: Uses specific kinship terms 'A-peh' and 'A-m' along with the second person plural 'lín'.

items.title: 天津卫学闽南方言の章度总结
items.transcript: 我欲去加樓下樓阿伯樓阿姆拜年。
items.translation_en: I want to go give New Year greetings to the uncle and auntie downstairs.
items.description: Features 'beh' (want/will) and 'kā' (preposition/marker) characteristic of Min Nan grammar.

items.title: 江淮官话版《燕归巢》扬州话版
items.transcript: 雨后江岸天破晓
items.translation_en: Dawn breaks on the riverbank after the rain
items.description: The pronunciation of 'Xiao' (晓) displays the specific dipthong characteristics found in Yangzhou dialect phonology.

items.title: 江淮官话版《燕归巢》扬州话版
items.transcript: 老宅新客自独扫
items.translation_en: A new guest sweeps the old house alone
items.description: The words 'Zhai' (宅) and 'Ke' (客) are pronounced with the short, clipped entry tone (Ru Sheng) typical of Jianghuai Mandarin.

items.title: 江淮官话版《燕归巢》扬州话版
items.transcript: 燕未归，灯罢冬晓，烟村笛声归草
items.translation_en: The swallows haven't returned, the lamps are extinguished at winter dawn, the sound of the flute in the misty village returns to the grass.
items.description: Vowel variations in 'Xiao' and 'Cao' reflect the local phonetic system of the Yangzhou area.

items.title: 江淮官话版《燕归巢》扬州话版
items.transcript: 柳絮长，几多欢郎，庭前儿女旧游方
items.translation_en: The willow catkins are long, how many happy young men, children in front of the court are still traveling.
items.description: The nasal endings in 'Lang' (郎) and 'Fang' (方) are distinctive of the regional Jianghuai accent.

items.title: 【河北】唐山话入门100句
items.transcript: 捧谢
items.translation_en: No need for thanks.
items.description: Uses the dialect-specific contraction 'beng' (a fusion of 'bu' and 'yong') instead of the standard 'bu yong xie'.

items.title: 【河北】唐山话入门100句
items.transcript: 这么巧啊？你上哪儿且？
items.translation_en: What a coincidence! Where are you going?
items.description: The pronunciation of 'qu' (去) as 'qie' is a signature phonetic feature of the Tangshan dialect.

items.title: 【河北】唐山话入门100句
items.transcript: 我屑你
items.translation_en: I like you / I care about you.
items.description: Uses 'xie' (屑) as a local synonym for 'like' (喜欢), which is very common in Tangshan vernacular.

items.title: 【河北】唐山话入门100句
items.transcript: 你咋么呢捏啊
items.translation_en: Why are you being like this?
items.description: Includes the sentence-final particle 'nie', which is a quintessential marker of the Tangshan accent and dialect.

items.title: 【河北】唐山话入门100句
items.transcript: 中啊
items.translation_en: Okay / Sure.
items.description: While 'zhong' is often associated with Henan, it is also heavily used in Tangshan and other northern regions to express agreement or adequacy.

items.title: 珠海人讲四邑话，似台山开平新会恩平
items.transcript: 大家好，我叫阿源，来自珠海干务的00后。我讲的话都系属于四邑话的一个分支。
items.translation_en: Hello everyone, I'm A-Yuan, born in the 2000s from Ganwu, Zhuhai. The language I speak belongs to a branch of the Siyi (Four Cities) dialect.
items.description: The guest introduces himself using the distinct phonetics of the Doumen/Siyi accent, particularly noticeable in the pronunciation of 'Zhuhai' and 'Siyi'.

items.title: 珠海人讲四邑话，似台山开平新会恩平
items.transcript: 因为我讲嗰种话呢，我住嗰个村呢，叫做北水，我哋嗰度就叫北水话，但大部分人都系叫斗门话或者四邑话。
items.translation_en: Regarding the language I speak, the village I live in is called Beishui, so we call it Beishui dialect, but most people call it Doumen dialect or Siyi dialect.
items.description: The speaker uses specific local terminology for his village's dialect and exhibits the characteristic 'Siyi-wa' (Four Cities) tonal shifts.

items.title: 珠海人讲四邑话，似台山开平新会恩平
items.transcript: 平常用既，离新会也好，三会也好，都唔系话太远。平时的沟通都可以大概听得明。
items.translation_en: The language we usually use isn't that far from Xinhui or San-hui. In daily communication, we can basically understand each other.
items.description: The mention of Xinhui and the specific tonal markers on 'San-hui' highlight the regional dialectal group (Siyi).

items.title: 珠海人讲四邑话，似台山开平新会恩平
items.transcript: 唔系好识讲，但平时简单的词听得明。好似四邑话咁，听得个七八成左右。
items.translation_en: I can't speak it very well, but I can understand simple words. Just like the Siyi dialect, I can understand about 70 to 80 percent.
items.description: The guest discusses his proficiency in related sub-dialects while maintaining the local accent's specific vowel qualities.

items.title: 珠海人讲四邑话，似台山开平新会恩平
items.transcript: 系呀，广州话。但我同我妈妈，即系我妈妈嗰辈，讲北水村的人讲既，就系讲我头先讲嗰种话。
items.translation_en: Yes, Cantonese. But with my mother, that is my mother's generation, people from Beishui village speak the kind of language I spoke just now.
items.description: The speaker contrasts standard Cantonese ('Guangzhou-wa') with his local village dialect, using regional sentence endings.

items.title: 合肥话版撒西不理来袭！禁欲系演绎，这味儿太上头了！
items.transcript: 合肥大VS卷谁
items.translation_en: Who is Hefei big VS rolling with?
items.description: Features the local pronunciation of 'Hefei' and uses 'Juan' in a context typical of modern regional slang.

items.title: 合肥话版撒西不理来袭！禁欲系演绎，这味儿太上头了！
items.transcript: 外一哈离撮死人
items.translation_en: Just a little bit is extremely annoying.
items.description: Includes the classic Hefei dialect intensifier '撮死人' (cuo si ren).

items.title: 合肥话版撒西不理来袭！禁欲系演绎，这味儿太上头了！
