items.translation_en: But in the Guilin dialect, the 'entering tone' is completely categorized into the 'yangping' tone, which is the most iconic feature of Ming Dynasty Mandarin.
items.description: Describes a specific phonological feature (tone evolution) unique to the dialect's history.

items.title: #桂林话明朝味儿#芒果奇石#春色奇石#漓江竹林深奇石#桂林文化
items.transcript: 桂林话，是明代通语的声音活化石，是曾经的中国标准音，留在西南的时间胶囊。
items.translation_en: The Guilin dialect is a vocal living fossil of the Ming Dynasty's common language; it is a time capsule of what was once China's standard pronunciation, preserved in the southwest.
items.description: Summarizes the dialect as a 'living fossil' and 'time capsule' of historical Chinese phonetics.

items.title: 【江淮官话】马鞍山方言an怎么读最地道？
items.transcript: 发，切发
items.translation_en: Rice, eat rice
items.description: The 'an' final in 'fan' shifts to an 'a' sound, and 'chi' is pronounced as 'qie', typical of Jianghuai Mandarin.

items.title: 【江淮官话】马鞍山方言an怎么读最地道？
items.transcript: 妈，他妈个
items.translation_en: Slow, he is slow
items.description: The final 'an' in 'man' is simplified to an 'a' vowel in this dialect.

items.title: 【江淮官话】马鞍山方言an怎么读最地道？
items.transcript: 盆子碟子
items.translation_en: Pots and saucers
items.description: The nasal final 'en' in 'pen' shifts towards a rounded 'ong' sound.

items.title: 【江淮官话】马鞍山方言an怎么读最地道？
items.transcript: 金，金卡
items.translation_en: See, look
items.description: The final 'ian' in 'jian' shifts to a raised 'in' sound.

items.title: 【江淮官话】马鞍山方言an怎么读最地道？
items.transcript: 从，从衣裳
items.translation_en: Wear, wear clothes
items.description: The final 'uan' in 'chuan' shifts to an 'ong' sound, a distinct feature of the local phonology.

items.title: 这次的东莞话小课堂 .我拉了一炮大的!!
items.transcript: 嘈有候, 有缘候, 呢两个词喺东莞话都系表示嘈吵同埋扰人嘅意思
items.translation_en: 'Chou You Hou' and 'You Yuan Hou', these two words in Dongguan dialect both mean noisy and disturbing.
items.description: These are specific local vocabulary terms used to describe noise and disturbance, typical of the Dongguan region.

items.title: 这次的东莞话小课堂 .我拉了一炮大的!!
items.transcript: 跂够脚系指以脚尖支撑而脚跟离地
items.translation_en: 'Qi Gou Jiao' refers to supporting oneself with the toes while the heels are off the ground.
items.description: The term 'Qi Gou Jiao' (standing on tiptoes) is a distinct regional expression for a physical action.

items.title: 这次的东莞话小课堂 .我拉了一炮大的!!
items.transcript: 滚无滚屎, 早期应该系喺呢啲乡里入面大人闹啲细路揾啲禽畜嘅毛同埋屎
items.translation_en: 'Gun Wu Gun Shi', originally it should have been adults in the villages scolding children for messing with livestock feathers and droppings.
items.description: This is a unique colloquial phrase with a specific local origin and meaning related to causing trouble or messing around.

items.title: 这次的东莞话小课堂 .我拉了一炮大的!!
items.transcript: 打电呀, 又可以话打电打呀, 系指讲多余嘅说话, 做多余嘅事
items.translation_en: 'Da Dian Ya', also can be said as 'Da Dian Da Ya', refers to saying redundant things or doing redundant tasks.
items.description: The idiomatic expression 'Da Dian' is specific to the Dongguan dialect to describe redundancy in speech or action.

items.title: 这次的东莞话小课堂 .我拉了一炮大的!!
items.transcript: 负老黄, 呢个词相对嚟讲可能好多人都未听过, 对应嘅系我哋东莞话嘅放负老
items.translation_en: 'Fu Lao Huang', many people might not have heard this word; it corresponds to our Dongguan dialect's 'Fang Fu Lao'.
items.description: Identifies 'Fu Lao Huang' as a highly specific local term used for bragging or talking nonsense.

items.title: 香港围头话例句展示  粤语莞宝片方言
items.transcript: 你叫咩名？你喺邊度？
items.translation_en: What is your name? Where are you?
items.description: Waitau pronunciation uses distinct tones and vowel shifts compared to standard Hong Kong Cantonese, notably in the words 'name' and 'where'.

items.title: 香港围头话例句展示  粤语莞宝片方言
items.transcript: 你喺哪一個？尋晚一晚，你三三聲喂吓。
items.translation_en: Who are you? Last night, you kept shouting 'hey' repeatedly.
items.description: Uses specific Waitau phrasing like '哪一個' for 'who' and the repetitive '三三聲' to describe persistent noise.

items.title: 香港围头话例句展示  粤语莞宝片方言
items.transcript: 唔好咁大脾氣。打火機、煎蛋、同埋嗰冷氣。唔使急，你等我一陣。
items.translation_en: Don't have such a big temper. Lighter, fried eggs, and the air conditioner. No need to rush, wait for me a moment.
items.description: Features the Waitau accent's characteristic treatment of dental and velar consonants in words like 'eggs' and 'air conditioner'.

items.title: 香港围头话例句展示  粤语莞宝片方言
items.transcript: 我今日去行街買咗部新嘅單車飛輪。有咩高見可以話畀我聽？
items.translation_en: I went shopping today and bought a new bicycle flywheel. Do you have any opinions to tell me?
items.description: Includes '飛輪' (flywheel) and the Waitau-inflected '話畀我聽', showing the tonal variation in the local dialect.

items.title: 香港围头话例句展示  粤语莞宝片方言
items.transcript: 唉，老竇，幾多年唔見你竟然變成咁樣？仲要我講幾多次呀，點解你唔可以做出一些改變呢？
items.translation_en: Oh, Dad, how many years has it been and you've turned into this? How many more times do I have to say it, why can't you make some changes?
items.description: The speaker demonstrates emotional cadence in Waitau, highlighting the specific pitch contours of the Guan-Bao dialect group.

items.title: 【大肉嘎】主播合肥话只有高中水准
items.transcript: 他里面用了一个词，叫上人。
items.translation_en: He used a word in there called 'shangren'.
items.description: The speaker introduces a specific dialect term used by a local driver.

items.title: 【大肉嘎】主播合肥话只有高中水准
items.transcript: 上人是在合肥话里面是父母的意思，长辈的意思。
items.translation_en: 'Shangren' in Hefei dialect means parents or elders.
items.description: He explains the specific meaning of a Hefei dialect vocabulary item.

items.title: 【大肉嘎】主播合肥话只有高中水准
items.transcript: 老头，老娘，爸爸，嫲嫲。
items.translation_en: Father, mother, dad, mom.
items.description: He lists informal kinship terms with characteristic dialectal pronunciation.

items.title: 【大肉嘎】主播合肥话只有高中水准
items.transcript: 马子，哎对对对，马子，老头，老头，马子。
items.translation_en: Mazi, oh right, right, right, mazi, old man, old man, mazi.
items.description: He uses 'mazi', which in local Hefei context is a dialectal term for a female elder or mother.

items.title: 【大肉嘎】主播合肥话只有高中水准
items.transcript: 合肥腔首先是前后鼻音分的不开，几乎没有什么后鼻音。
items.translation_en: The Hefei accent first of all doesn't distinguish between front and back nasal sounds; there's almost no back nasal.
items.description: The speaker describes a core phonological characteristic of the Hefei dialect accent.

items.title: 中国最悦耳的云南方言，丽江曲靖话唯美动听，昆明海莱腔百听不厌
items.transcript: 难益干时啦，都道他家有一得古丽。
items.translation_en: It's hard to explain, everyone says there's a lovely girl in his family.
items.description: This is a characteristic Lijiang dialect phrase with unique phonetics and vocabulary like 'nan yi gan shi'.

items.title: 中国最悦耳的云南方言，丽江曲靖话唯美动听，昆明海莱腔百听不厌
items.transcript: 蝴蝶自左扎吗，扎出来吗下自左扎，扎出来吗菜出来吗。
items.translation_en: This is how you tie the butterfly, tie it like this and it comes out like a flower.
items.description: The speaker uses a strong local Yunnan accent, particularly in the rhythmic repetition of 'za ma' (tie/make).

items.title: 中国最悦耳的云南方言，丽江曲靖话唯美动听，昆明海莱腔百听不厌
items.transcript: 比如说我们叫膝盖是叫棵膝泰，在南京方言里面也是叫做可膝头。
items.translation_en: For example, we call the knee 'Ke Xi Tai', which is very similar to 'Ke Xi Tou' in the Nanjing dialect.
items.description: The speaker explicitly compares the local Kunming/Yunnan word for knee with the Nanjing dialect, showing linguistic connection.

items.title: 中国最悦耳的云南方言，丽江曲靖话唯美动听，昆明海莱腔百听不厌
items.transcript: 当初我是不要你，是你自己找上门，如今你说我不好，只有选材去你回。
items.translation_en: I didn't want you at first, you came to me yourself; now you say I'm no good, so I have to send you back.
items.description: This rap is performed in a thick Kunming-area dialect, noticeable in the tones and 'er' sounds.

items.title: 中国最悦耳的云南方言，丽江曲靖话唯美动听，昆明海莱腔百听不厌
items.transcript: 洛博阿玛拿火撇角，多拉哟。
items.translation_en: [Singing in ethnic minority dialect/language, likely Yi or Hani]
items.description: The performer uses an ethnic minority language/dialect from Yunnan, characteristic of high-pitched mountain folk songs.

items.title: 回忆北京方言·270·褪
items.transcript: 褪色儿
items.translation_en: Fading colors
items.description: Uses the Erhua sound 'shar' and the specific Beijing pronunciation 'tui' for color fading.

items.title: 回忆北京方言·270·褪
items.transcript: 褪毛，用开水浇了褪毛
items.translation_en: Removing fur; pouring boiling water to remove hair/feathers.
items.description: Demonstrates the pronunciation 'tui' in the context of removing fur or hair from animals.

items.title: 回忆北京方言·270·褪
items.transcript: 往后褪，往后，往后
items.translation_en: Move backward, back, back.
items.description: The pronunciation 'tun' used here to mean moving backward is a distinct feature of the Beijing dialect.

items.title: 回忆北京方言·270·褪
items.transcript: 把手褪袖口里头
items.translation_en: Tucking hands into the sleeves.
items.description: Uses 'tun' to mean tucking something inside, a common usage in Northern and Beijing dialects.
